583	% $$
584	% ... We compute ... $(dh + hd)(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ ...
585	% This proves the identity map is homotopic to zero as desired."
586	\begin{proof}
587	  \uses{def:cech_complex, def:standard_affine_cover}
588	  Write \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) for an
589	  \(A\)-module \(M\).
590	
591	  \paragraph{Identification of the abstract {\v C}ech complex with the localisation
592	  complex (the categorical--module bridge).}
593	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Lemma \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
594	  % items (4)--(5) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L691--719).
595	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "(4) For every $f\in R$ we have $\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde M) = M_f$
596	  % as an $R_f$-module.
597	  % (5) Whenever $D(g) \subset D(f)$ the restriction mappings
598	  % on $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$ and $\widetilde M$
599	  % are the maps $R_f \to R_g$ and $M_f \to M_g$ from Lemma
600	  % \ref{lemma-standard-open}."
601	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
602	  items (4)--(5).}
603	  The {\v C}ech complex of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} is built from the
604	  \emph{abstract} cover \(\mathcal{U} = (\text{affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}\;
605	  s\; hs).\mathrm{openCover}\) of Definition~\ref{def:standard_affine_cover}, whose
606	  degree-\(p\) term is an object of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} A)\); we
607	  record explicitly how those terms and their differential become the concrete
608	  complex of localised modules that the homotopy argument operates on (writing
609	  \(s_i = f_i\) for the spanning family). First, the \(i\)-th piece of
610	  \(\mathcal{U}\) is \(\operatorname{Spec}(A_{s_i}) \to \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the
611	  open immersion of the basic open \(D(s_i)\); the \((p+1)\)-fold intersection
612	  indexed by a tuple \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) is the basic open
613	  \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
614	  \[
615	    s_\sigma \;=\; \prod_{k=0}^{p} s_{\sigma(k)}
616	      \;=\; s_{\sigma(0)} \cdots s_{\sigma(p)},
617	  \]
618	  because the basic opens of an affine satisfy \(D(s_{i_0}) \cap \cdots \cap
619	  D(s_{i_p}) = D(s_{i_0} \cdots s_{i_p})\); its sections form the \emph{away}
620	  localisation \(A_{s_\sigma} = A_{s_{\sigma(0)} \cdots s_{\sigma(p)}}\). Second,
621	  because \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) is quasi-coherent, item~(4) of the cited
622	  lemma identifies its sections over each basic open with the away localisation of
623	  the module,
624	  \[
625	    \Gamma\bigl(D(s_\sigma), \mathcal{F}\bigr) \;=\; M_{s_\sigma},
626	  \]
627	  the localisation of \(M\) at the multiplicative set generated by the product
628	  \(s_\sigma\) (the \(\operatorname{Localization.Away}\) /
629	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) localisation of \(M\) at \(s_\sigma\)). Hence
630	  the degree-\(p\) term of the abstract {\v C}ech complex is, term by term, the
631	  product of away localisations
632	  \[
633	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
634	      \;=\; \prod_{\sigma : \{0,\ldots,p\} \to \iota}
635	              \Gamma\bigl(D(s_\sigma), \mathcal{F}\bigr)
636	      \;=\; \prod_{\sigma : \{0,\ldots,p\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma}.
637	  \]
638	  Third, the {\v C}ech differential of the abstract complex --- the alternating sum
639	  \(\sum_{j=0}^{p+1}(-1)^j\) of the restriction maps along the face inclusions
640	  \(D(s_\sigma) \hookrightarrow D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})\) obtained by omitting the
641	  \(j\)-th index (so \(D(s_\sigma) \subset D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})\), the product
642	  \(s_\sigma\) carrying the extra factor \(s_{\sigma(j)}\)) --- is, by item~(5) of
643	  the cited lemma (the restriction of \(\widetilde M\) along an inclusion of basic
644	  opens \(D(g) \subset D(f)\) is precisely the localisation map \(M_f \to M_g\)),
645	  exactly the alternating sum of the localisation maps
646	  \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\) between the away localisations. The
647	  degreewise identification of the second step therefore intertwines the abstract
648	  {\v C}ech coboundary with the concrete localised coboundary: it is an isomorphism
649	  of cochain complexes from \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
650	  onto the concrete complex of away localisations. Consequently the positive-degree
651	  vanishing \(\operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)\)
652	  of the abstract complex is equivalent to positive-degree exactness of the concrete
653	  localised complex, and it is the latter that the local-to-global criterion and the
654	  contracting homotopy below establish.
655	
656	  By Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} the {\v C}ech complex of
657	  the standard cover is therefore the complex of localisations
658	  \[
659	    \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \;\to\;
660	    \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \;\to\;
661	    \prod_{i_0 i_1 i_2} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1} f_{i_2}} \;\to\; \cdots,
662	  \]
663	  and the claim \(\check{H}^p = 0\) for \(p > 0\) is equivalent to exactness of the
664	  extended complex \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \cdots\) (whose
665	  truncation is studied in Algebra, Lemma \texttt{algebra-lemma-cover-module}).
666	  Exactness may be checked after localising at an arbitrary prime
667	  \(\mathfrak{p} \subset A\) (Algebra, Lemma
668	  \texttt{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}). Choose an index
669	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) with \(f_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \notin \mathfrak{p}\); then
670	  \(M_{f_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}, \mathfrak{p}} = M_{\mathfrak{p}}\), and the
671	  prescription \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\)
672	  defines a contracting homotopy of the localised extended complex: a direct
673	  computation gives \((dh + hd)(s) = s\), so the identity is homotopic to zero and
674	  the localised complex is exact. As exactness holds after every localisation, the
675	  extended complex is exact and \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for
676	  \(p > 0\).
677	
678	  Concretely, exactness is established node-by-node from the spanning family
679	  \((s, hs)\) of Definition~\ref{def:standard_affine_cover}: the local-to-global
680	  exactness criterion for a span of the unit ideal localises the complex at the
681	  spanning elements (the away localisations \(A_{s_r}\)), reducing positive-degree
682	  exactness of the extended complex to exactness of each localised complex; in the
683	  localisation \(A_{s_r}\) the fixed index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} = r\) makes
684	  \(s_r\) invertible, so the contracting homotopy
685	  \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{r\, i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is defined globally on that
686	  localisation and contracts it.
687	\end{proof}
688	
689	\subsection{Presheaf-level {\v C}ech machinery}
690	\label{sec:presheaf_cech_machinery}
691	
692	The {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
693	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} below) is not a statement about a single
694	affine cover: it is proved on the abelian category \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
695	of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, where the {\v C}ech complex of sections
696	is the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex of an explicit free resolution. The argument
697	rests on two presheaf-level facts, recorded here, and one transfer of injectivity.
698	First, a free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of presheaves of modules whose
699	\(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex into \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{is} the {\v C}ech complex
700	of sections (Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex},
701	Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and
702	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification}). Second, the resolution property of
703	that complex: \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) resolves \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\)
704	concentrated in degree \(0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso}). Together
705	these give: an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is injective as a presheaf
706	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} via
707	Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} applied to the sheafification adjunction
708	Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), so \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) is
709	exact and carries the augmented resolution to an exact section complex, i.e.\
710	\(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\). This direct route uses
711	neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
712	\(\delta\)-functor identification.
713	
714	\begin{definition}
715	[Free presheaf complex of a cover]
716	  \label{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
717	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex}
718	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into}
719	  % (the complex $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$) (read from
720	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1142--1162, with the $j_{p!}$ description
721	  % L1095--1135).
722	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Consider the chain complex $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$
723	  % of presheaves of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules
724	  % $$
725	  % \ldots
726	  % \to
727	  % \bigoplus_{i_0i_1i_2} (j_{i_0i_1i_2})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0i_1i_2}}
728	  % \to
729	  % \bigoplus_{i_0i_1} (j_{i_0i_1})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0i_1}}
730	  % \to
731	  % \bigoplus_{i_0} (j_{i_0})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0}}
732	  % \to 0 \to \ldots
733	  % $$
734	  % where the last nonzero term is placed in degree $0$
735	  % and where the map
736	  % $$
737	  % (j_{i_0\ldots i_{p + 1}})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0\ldots i_{p + 1}}}
738	  % \longrightarrow
739	  % (j_{i_0\ldots \hat i_j \ldots i_{p + 1}})_{p!}
740	  % \mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0\ldots \hat i_j \ldots i_{p + 1}}}
741	  % $$
742	  % is given by $(-1)^j$ times the canonical map."
743	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
744	  \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into} (the free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\)).}
745	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an
746	  open covering, with \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\)
747	  the open immersion of each finite intersection. The Stacks formulation uses, for
748	  each intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\), the \emph{extension-by-zero} presheaf
749	  \((j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{!}\,\mathcal{O}_X|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\), the presheaf of
750	  modules sending an open \(W\) to \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when
751	  \(W \subset U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) and to \(0\) otherwise. This extension-by-zero
752	  presheaf is, canonically, the \emph{free presheaf of modules on the representable}
753	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\): writing
754	  \(\mathbf{y}\,V = \operatorname{Hom}(-, V)\) for the Yoneda presheaf of the open
755	  \(V\) on the site of opens of \(X\), the free presheaf of modules
756	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V)\) sends \(W\) to the free
757	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\)-module on the set \(\operatorname{Hom}(W, V)\), which is
758	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) if \(W \subset V\) and \(0\) otherwise — exactly
759	  extension-by-zero of \(\mathcal{O}_X|_V\). Its defining universal property
760	  \[
761	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}\bigl(
762	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V),\, \mathcal{F}\bigr)
763	    \;\cong\; \mathcal{F}(V)
764	  \]
765	  is the free–forgetful adjunction for presheaves of modules composed with the
766	  Yoneda isomorphism \(\operatorname{Hom}(\mathbf{y}\,V, G) \cong G(V)\). The
767	  \emph{free presheaf complex} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the chain complex
768	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) in the abelian category
769	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose
770	  term in degree \(p\) is the direct sum
771	  \[
772	    K(\mathcal{U})_p
773	      = \bigoplus_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
774	        \operatorname{free}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
775	  \]

1	# Analogy: representing & proving P3 (standard-cover Čech acyclicity on affines)
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment

11	
12	## Question
13	
14	How should the project represent and prove **P3** — positive-degree exactness of the
15	standard-cover Čech complex of an affine scheme — for `Scheme.Modules`? Two coupled
16	decisions: (a) the Lean *encoding* of "a standard affine open cover of `Spec A`"
17	(bespoke `Fin n` structure / predicate on an `OpenCover` / Mathlib affine-cover API);
18	(b) how to represent the complex-and-exactness: (1) does Mathlib already have the
19	algebra-level Čech-of-localizations exactness (`Algebra.lemma-cover-module`)? (2) the
20	idiomatic "exact after localizing at every prime" certifier? (3) contracting homotopy
21	via `Homotopy`/`HomotopyEquiv` on a `CochainComplex`, or via an augmented-simplicial
22	extra degeneracy Mathlib already knows is exact?
23	
24	## Project artifact(s)
25	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean:764-774` — `CechAcyclic.affine`
26	  (the P3 `sorry`; currently a *general* `𝒰 : X.OpenCover` signature). NOT protected.
27	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean:737-745` — `CechComplex`
28	  (takes general `OpenCover`; built abstractly via `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`).
29	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean:801-811` —
30	  `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (**PROTECTED**, general `𝒰 : X.OpenCover`).
31	- `blueprint/.../Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex` — `lem:cech_acyclic_affine`,
32	  `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`, `lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis`.
33	
34	## Decisions identified
35	
36	### Decision (a): encoding of "standard affine cover of Spec A"
37	
38	- **Mathlib idiom**: `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop`
39	  (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open`):
40	  ```
41	  affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop {R : CommRingCat} {ι : Type*}
42	    (s : ι → R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) : (Spec R).AffineOpenCover
43	  ```
44	  with the proven defining equation
45	  `affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop_f : (… ).f i = Spec.map (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (s i)))`
46	  and the projection `AffineOpenCover.openCover : X.OpenCover`. So Mathlib already
47	  encodes "standard cover of `Spec R` from a spanning family" as exactly the data
48	  `(s : ι → R, hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)`. Supporting API:
49	  `PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff' : (⨆ i ∈ s, basicOpen i = ⊤) ↔ Ideal.span s = ⊤`
50	  and `IsAffineOpen.self_le_iSup_basicOpen_iff`.
51	- **Project's current path**: general `𝒰 : X.OpenCover` on `CechAcyclic.affine`; the
52	  directive proposes narrowing. Of the three options floated: (i) bespoke
53	  `(f : Fin n → A) + hspan`; (ii) predicate on an existing `OpenCover`; (iii) Mathlib's
54	  affine-cover API.
55	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost for options (i)/(ii); option (iii) is identical to idiom.
56	- **Cost of divergence**: the decisive point is that the **same** spanning-family bundle
57	  `(s, hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)` is what the algebra-side certifier
58	  `exact_of_isLocalized_span` *also* consumes (see (b2)) — it wants `Ideal.span s = ⊤`
59	  and `IsLocalizedModule.Away (s i)`. So if `CechAcyclic.affine` carries `(s, hs)` and
60	  builds the cover via `affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs |>.openCover`, one data
61	  bundle drives **both** the geometry (cover) and the algebra (exactness) with zero
62	  bridge lemmas. A bespoke `Fin n` structure (option i) would need two conversion lemmas
63	  (to `affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop` and to the `exact_of_isLocalized_span` shape).
64	  A predicate on an arbitrary `OpenCover` (option ii) is worst: it forces a
65	  "this cover is iso to the standard one" comparison at every use site and re-derives
66	  the `D(f_i)`-structure that Mathlib already packages.
67	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB (option iii). Take `(s : ι → Γ(X,O), hs)` as the
68	  hypotheses; obtain the cover from `affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop`.
69	- **Frozen-signature note**: `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` is protected with a
70	  general `𝒰 : X.OpenCover`, and `CechComplex` takes a general `OpenCover` — both stay
71	  as-is. The narrowing is ONLY on the non-protected `CechAcyclic.affine`. This is
72	  consistent with the existing blueprint design: the protected consumer reaches affine
73	  acyclicity through the basis-comparison on *standard* covers
74	  (`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` / `affine_serre_vanishing`), not through the user's
75	  `𝒰` directly, so `CechAcyclic.affine` is free to be stated for the standard cover.
76	
77	### Decision (b1): does Mathlib have the algebra Čech-of-localizations exactness?
78	
79	- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE. Searched leansearch/loogle for the `Algebra.lemma-cover-module`
80	  analogue — exactness of `0 → M → ∏ M_{f_i} → ∏ M_{f_i f_j} → ⋯`. Mathlib has only:
81	  the degree-≤1 *sheaf condition* for the structure sheaf
82	  (`TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheafEqualizerProducts`, `StructureSheaf` gluing, structure-sheaf
83	  `objSupIsoProdEqLocus`), which is the `M → ∏ M_{f_i} ⇉ ∏ M_{f_i f_j}` *equalizer*
84	  (kernel/H⁰+H¹ only), and the local-to-global certifiers of (b2). The full positive-degree
85	  complex exactness is absent.
86	- **Project's current path**: build it from scratch (the `CechAcyclic.affine` sorry).
87	- **Gap**: divergent-and-necessary — there is no idiom to align to here.
88	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL. This is the genuinely from-scratch core of P3.
89	
90	### Decision (b2): "exact after localizing" certifier
91	
92	- **Mathlib idiom** (HIGHEST-VALUE FIND): `Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalProperties.Exactness`:
93	  ```
94	  exact_of_isLocalized_span (s : Set R) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)
95	    (Mₚ Nₚ Lₚ : ↑s → Type*) … (f r : IsLocalizedModule.Away ↑r) …
96	    (F : M →ₗ[R] N) (G : N →ₗ[R] L) :
97	    (∀ r : s, Function.Exact (map (powers ↑r) (f r) (g r) F)
98	                             (map (powers ↑r) (g r) (h r) G)) →
99	    Function.Exact ⇑F ⇑G
100	  ```
101	  This is the Mathlib analogue of Stacks `algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local`, but
102	  localizing at the **spanning elements** (`Away ↑r` = `M_{f_r}`) rather than at primes —
103	  a strictly better match to the Čech setting. Sibling `exact_of_isLocalized_maximal`
104	  (localize at every maximal ideal, `AtPrime`) is the literal prime-local version;
105	  `exact_of_localized_span` / `exact_of_localized_maximal` are the `Localization`-typed
106	  variants. Also present: `injective_/surjective_/bijective_of_isLocalized_span` and the
107	  `_maximal` family.
108	- **Project's current path**: blueprint proof currently phrased via primes
109	  ("localising at an arbitrary prime 𝔭", Stacks). The element-localization certifier is
110	  cleaner and Mathlib-native.
111	- **Gap**: identical (this IS the idiom for local-to-global exactness).
112	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB. Apply `exact_of_isLocalized_span` with `s = Set.range f`
113	  node-by-node: positive-degree homology vanishing of the cochain complex at degree `p`
114	  is `Function.Exact (d^{p-1}) (d^p)`; reduce it to `Function.Exact` of each `Away (f_r)`
115	  localization. After inverting `f_r` itself, the cover member `D(f_r)` is the whole space,
116	  so the contracting homotopy with `i_fix = r` exists *globally on the localization* — no
117	  passage to a prime needed. (Needs: localization of the finite product term commutes,
118	  `(∏_σ M_{f_σ})_{f_r} ≅ ∏_σ M_{f_σ f_r}` — available via `IsLocalizedModule`/finite `Pi`,
119	  e.g. `IsLocalization.instForallPiUniv`.)
120	
121	### Decision (b3): contracting-homotopy idiom
122	
123	- **Mathlib idiom**: `CategoryTheory.SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy` +
124	  `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicTopology.ExtraDegeneracy`): an extra
125	  degeneracy on an augmented **simplicial** object yields a `HomotopyEquiv` between its
126	  `AlternatingFaceMapComplex` (a *chain* complex) and the point — precisely the Stacks
127	  homotopy `h(s)_{i₀…i_p} = s_{i_fix i₀…i_p}`. BUT two mismatches:
128	  (1) it is the **simplicial**/chain-complex (`AlternatingFaceMapComplex`) statement; the
129	      project's `CechComplex` is **cosimplicial** + `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` (a
130	      *cochain* complex), and Mathlib has **no cosimplicial dual** of `ExtraDegeneracy`;
131	  (2) there is no *global* `i_fix` — the extra degeneracy exists only after localizing at
132	      `f_{i_fix}`.
133	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost if routed through the simplicial API; the dualization plus
134	  the global-vs-local mismatch makes a direct dependency heavy.
135	- **Verdict**: PARTIAL → NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (build a small local homotopy, don't port).
136	  Recommendation: do the contracting homotopy at the **algebra/module level** on the
137	  localized cochain complex of `M_{f_σ f_r}`'s — either as a `CategoryTheory.Homotopy` on
138	  the `CochainComplex`, or (simpler) just prove the 3-term `Function.Exact` per node from
139	  the explicit `dh + hd = id` computation and feed `exact_of_isLocalized_span`. The
140	  explicit module homotopy is a few lines and sidesteps the cosimplicial-extra-degeneracy
141	  infrastructure Mathlib lacks. Keep `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` in mind as the
142	  *template* for the formula, not as a code dependency.
143	
144	## Recommendation
145	
146	Narrow `CechAcyclic.affine` to carry the spanning-family data `(s : ι → A, hs :
147	Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)` and build its cover with
148	`affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs |>.openCover` (Decision a, ALIGN). The protected
149	`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` / `CechComplex` keep their general-`OpenCover`
150	signatures untouched — the basis-comparison route already isolates standard covers, so
151	only the non-protected `CechAcyclic.affine` changes. For the proof, the three-layer
152	bridge is: (L1, gap-fill) identify `CechComplex` on the standard cover with the module
153	complex `∏_σ M_{f_σ}` via `Γ(D(f_σ)) = M_{f_σ}`; (L2, ALIGN) feed each positive-degree
154	node to `exact_of_isLocalized_span` with `s = Set.range f`; (L3, gap-fill) supply the
155	explicit `Away (f_r)`-local contracting homotopy `h(s)_{i₀…i_p} = s_{r i₀…i_p}` giving
156	`Function.Exact` of the localized differentials. Mathlib provides L2 and the cover
157	constructor outright — do NOT reinvent them; the genuinely from-scratch work is L1 (the
158	geometry↔algebra section-localization bridge) and the L3 module homotopy. Avoid routing
159	through Mathlib's *simplicial* `ExtraDegeneracy` — it is the wrong variance (chain vs.
160	cochain) and has no cosimplicial dual.

1	# Čech / Koszul / acyclic-cover precedent audit (iter-059)
2	
3	## Project context
4	
5	Iter-053–058 set up the infrastructure for a forthcoming Čech-acyclicity
6	proof on basic-open covers:
7	
8	- `class HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F` (iter-053) — for every affine `U`, the
9	  existence of a cover `𝒰` of `U` such that `[IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]` and
10	  `[HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰]`.
11	- `basicOpenCover s : s → Opens` and its supremum / membership /
12	  pairwise- / Finset-`inf'`-intersection identifications
13	  (iter-054 → iter-058).
14	- `class IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰` (iter-048) — positive-degree
15	  Čech cohomology subsingleton.
16	- `class HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰` (iter-050) — `Nonempty`-wrapped
17	  `∀ n, cechCohomology n ≃ₗ[k] HModule' k F n (⨆𝒰)`.
18	
19	The project now needs, for `F := toModuleKSheaf C`,
20	
21	1. `IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)` whenever
22	   `Ideal.span (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) = ⊤`.
23	2. `HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)` for the same
24	   `s`.
25	
26	Before committing iter-059+ to the project-local construction, this analogy
27	call audits Mathlib for prior art.
28	
29	## What `cechComplexFunctor` produces
30	
31	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean:65`
32	
33	```lean
34	noncomputable def cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ :=
35	  FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech
36	```
37	
38	Unfolding the constituents:
39	
40	- `FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor E = cosimplicialObjectFunctor E
41	  ⋙ AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex A`
42	  (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean:53`)
43	- `cosimplicialObjectFunctor E = evalOp C A ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj
44	  E.rightOp` (same file, L43)
45	- `(FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech.obj ⦋n⦌ = U.power (Fin (n+1))`, whose
46	  underlying object in `C` is `∏ᶜ (U ∘ i)` indexed by
47	  `i : Fin (n + 1) → ι` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/Cech.lean:34,186`)
48	
49	In our setting `C = (Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ`, `A = ModuleCat k`,
50	`P = (sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F`, finite products in `Opens` are infima.
51	The cochain at degree `n` is therefore exactly
52	
53	```
54	∏_{x : Fin (n+1) → ι} F(⨅ k : Fin (n+1), 𝒰 (x k))
55	```
56	
57	— a `Module k` product (not a Π-type) inside `ModuleCat.{u} k`.
58	The differential `d^n` is the standard alternating sum
59	`∑_{j : Fin (n+2)} (-1)^j · δ_j` (Mathlib's `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`,
60	`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/AlternatingFaceMapComplex.lean:67`).
61	
62	**Crucial caveat**: this is the *unaugmented* Čech complex. There is no
63	`F(U) → ∏_i F(𝒰 i)` term sitting in degree `-1`. Standard Čech-acyclicity
64	for affine covers is usually stated about the *augmented* complex (Stacks
65	01EJ / 03B1), so the project either has to build the augmentation
66	explicitly, or carry the equivalent reasoning at the level of the
67	unaugmented homology (using the sheaf axiom for degree 0).
68

86	### Precedent: `CategoryTheory.SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
87	
88	`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean:328`
89	
90	```lean
91	noncomputable def homotopyEquiv [Preadditive C] [HasZeroObject C]
92	    {X : SimplicialObject.Augmented C} (ed : ExtraDegeneracy X) :
93	    HomotopyEquiv (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.obj (drop.obj X))
94	      ((ChainComplex.single₀ C).obj (point.obj X))
95	```
96	
97	Augmented-simplicial-with-extra-degeneracy ⇒ alternating face map complex
98	is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on the augmentation. This
99	is the **engine** for proving that a Čech complex over a "good" cover is
100	null-homotopic in positive degrees: the work that would otherwise be the
101	alternating-sum-cancellation argument is encapsulated here. Note this is
102	on `AlternatingFaceMapComplex` (chain complex) rather than
103	`alternatingCofaceMapComplex` (cochain complex) — a dual will need to be
104	spelled out (or extracted by op).

106	### Precedent: `CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
107	
108	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean:92`
109	
110	```lean
111	noncomputable def extraDegeneracyCech {i₀ : U.I} (d : T ⟶ U.obj i₀) :
112	    (U.cech.augmentOfIsTerminal (isTerminalIncl _ hT)).ExtraDegeneracy
113	```
114	
115	Specialises `homotopyEquiv` to the formal-coproduct Čech object: given a
116	"vertex" — a section `T ⟶ U.obj i₀` of one of the cover terms from the
117	terminal — the augmented Čech object has an extra degeneracy. **This is
118	what fires after localizing at a single `f_i`**: in `D(f_i)`, the basic
119	open `D(f_i)` is a unit, so the affine `Spec(R[1/f_i]) = D(f_i)` is the
120	terminal of the cover-of-`D(f_i)` slice, and the inclusion `D(f_i) ⊂
121	D(f_i)` (i.e. the cover's `i₀ = i` member) supplies the section. Modulo
122	op-direction bookkeeping, this gives "Čech complex over `D(f_i)` is
123	null-homotopic in positive degrees" almost for free.

125	### Precedent: `exact_of_isLocalized_span`
126	
127	`Mathlib/RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean:173`
128	
129	```lean
130	lemma exact_of_isLocalized_span (H : ∀ r : s, Function.Exact
131	    (map (.powers r.1) (f r) (g r) F) (map (.powers r.1) (g r) (h r) G)) :
132	    Function.Exact F G
133	```
134	
135	(plus its sibling at L211, `exact_of_localized_span`, which directly
136	takes `f : M →ₗ N` without prequalifying localization data.)
137	
138	Hypothesis shape: for each generator `r ∈ s` with `span s = ⊤`,
139	`map (.powers r) (f r) (g r) F` (the induced map between localizations)
140	is composed with `map (.powers r) (g r) (h r) G` to be exact. Conclusion:
141	`F` and `G` are exact unlocalized.
142	
143	**This is the spanning-set Koszul-style local exactness lemma**. The
144	iter-057 correction (noting the project's iter-056 docstring referenced a
145	non-existent `exact_of_localized_span` without the "is" prefix) was an
146	overcorrection: both `exact_of_localized_span` and
147	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` exist; the former is the user-facing direct
148	form, the latter is the explicit-localization-data form. The iter-058
149	report should clarify both forms exist and which one the iter-059+ work
150	will actually call.

247	
248	## Cited references
249	
250	### Stacks Project §01ED (Čech cohomology)
251	
252	The acyclicity-on-affine proof in the Stacks Project (tag 01ED, sections
253	on Čech cohomology of quasicoherent sheaves on affine schemes) goes as
254	follows, *without* invoking Koszul:
255	
256	1. For an affine `U = Spec R`, a finite cover `𝒰 = (D(f_i))_i` with
257	   `(f_i)` generating the unit ideal of `R`, and a quasi-coherent `F`
258	   with `M = F(U)`, the augmented Čech complex
259	   `0 → M → ∏_i M[1/f_i] → ∏_{ij} M[1/f_i f_j] → ...` is exact.
260	
261	2. **Proof**: exactness is local on `D(f_i)` for each `i`. Localising at
262	   `f_i`, the augmented Čech complex over `D(f_i)` has `D(f_i)` as a cover
263	   member (`f_i` is a unit in `R[1/f_i]`), giving an extra degeneracy
264	   via "inclusion of the vertex". By the standard extra-degeneracy
265	   homotopy, the localised augmented complex is contractible, hence
266	   exact.
267	
268	3. By "exact on each open of a spanning cover ⇒ exact globally" (which
269	   is `exact_of_isLocalized_span` in Mathlib), the original augmented
270	   complex is exact.
271	
272	This route is the most economical for our setting: it requires *no*
273	Koszul-complex machinery; Mathlib's `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
274	encapsulates the alternating-sum cancellation; and the local-to-global
275	step is exactly `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
276	
277	### Stacks Project §03OU (Čech-to-cohomology spectral sequence)
278	
279	The general comparison `H^p(C^•(𝒰, F)) → H^p(U, F)` (Čech-to-derived
280	edge map) extends to a spectral sequence
281	`E^{p,q}_2 = Ȟ^p(𝒰, H^q(F)) ⇒ H^{p+q}(U, F)`. When the cover is acyclic
282	(all `H^q(intersection, F) = 0` for `q > 0`), the spectral sequence
283	degenerates and the edge map is an isomorphism. Mathlib does not have
284	this spectral sequence in any form. **For the project, the most
285	economical substitute** is to construct the edge map directly (without
286	the spectral sequence) by:
287	
288	- Producing the augmented Čech complex `C^• : 0 → F(U) → C^0 → C^1 → ...`
289	  on the affine. By step (1)–(3) above, this is acyclic, hence a
290	  resolution of `F(U)`.
291	- Sheaf-evaluating against an injective resolution of `F` (Mathlib does
292	  not provide injective sheaves in general, so this is the bottleneck).
293	- Identifying the resulting double-complex bipartite homology with
294	  `Ext`.
295	
296	Honest Lean blueprint estimate for the comparison: ~200-400 LOC.
297

306	- iter-054→058 basic-open identifications: `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`,
307	  `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`, `..._inter_eq_basicOpen_mul`,
308	  `..._finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod`, `..._finset_inf'_isAffineOpen`,

313	### What the project must provide
314	
315	#### Branch A — Čech acyclicity (~150-250 LOC)
316	
317	For each affine `U` and spanning `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`:
318	
319	1. **Augmentation lemma** (~30-50 LOC). Define the augmentation
320	   `F(U) → ∏_i F(𝒰 i)` as the natural sheaf-restriction morphism, and
321	   produce an *augmented* cochain complex extending iter-047's
322	   `Scheme.cechCochain`. This is structural; no Mathlib wrapper exists.
323	
324	2. **Localized identification** (~60-100 LOC). For each `f ∈ s`, identify
325	   the localization (at `f`) of the degree-`n` Čech cochain
326	   `∏_x F(⨅ k, 𝒰 (x k))` with the degree-`n` cochain of the
327	   "cover-of-`D(f)`" Čech complex. The key inputs are
328	   `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (each intersection
329	   identifies as `Localization.Away` of `Γ(U)`) and
330	   `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` (chain-level localization-preserves-exactness).
331	
332	3. **Extra-degeneracy invocation** (~30-60 LOC). The cover-of-`D(f)`
333	   Čech complex contains `D(f) = D(f ∩ f)` as a member, so applying
334	   `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` (or its op-dual for cochain
335	   complexes) gives an extra degeneracy on the augmented complex. By
336	   `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`, the augmented cochain complex over
337	   `D(f)` is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on `F(D(f))`,
338	   hence the (un-augmented) Čech complex over `D(f)` is exact in
339	   positive degrees.
340	
341	4. **Local-to-global** (~30-40 LOC). Per-degree, apply
342	   `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ...` with the per-`f` local exactness
343	   from step 3 to lift to exactness of the unlocalized differential.
344	   Repackage as `IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)`.
345	
346	#### Branch B — Čech-vs-derived comparison (~150-200 LOC)
347	
348	This is the substantial branch. Possibilities, ranked by economy:
349	
350	(i) **Direct comparison via augmented complex as resolution** (~150-200
351	LOC). After Branch A, the augmented Čech complex is exact, hence a
352	resolution of `F(U)`. Use `Abelian.Ext`'s functoriality (Mathlib has
353	`Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀` and adjacent API) to identify the homology of
354	`Hom(augmented Čech, F)` with `Ext^n(R, F)`.
355	
356	(ii) **Inductive Mayer-Vietoris** (~250-400 LOC). For finite covers,
357	unwind the cover one basic open at a time using the iter-014 LES.
358	Requires inductive structure and ad-hoc finite-cover reduction. **Not
359	recommended** — does more work and produces a less reusable artefact.
360	
361	(iii) **Spectral sequence formalisation** (~600+ LOC). Build Stacks 03OU
362	in Lean. **Strongly not recommended** — out of scope for the genus
363	proof.
364	
365	**Recommendation**: pursue (i). Total Branch B estimate ~150-200 LOC.
366	
367	### Total project-local LOC estimate
368	
369	~300-450 LOC across Branches A + B, spanning iter-059 to iter-066+.
370	This is within the directive's "~350-650 LOC" upper bound — the
371	mathlib-faithful design comes in at the low end of the plan-agent's

374	## Recommendation
375	
376	**Do not bypass the Čech-vs-derived comparison.** No Mathlib precedent
377	exists for "acyclic cover ⇒ Čech = Ext", but the constituent pieces —
378	`ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`, `extraDegeneracyCech`,
379	`exact_of_isLocalized_span`, `isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` — combine
380	into the standard Stacks 01ED proof of Čech acyclicity (Branch A) with
381	modest project-local glue (~150-250 LOC). The Čech-vs-derived comparison
382	(Branch B) is the real obligation; spending ~150-200 LOC on the direct
383	"augmented Čech complex is a resolution" route gives the cleanest
384	deliverable.
385	
386	**The alternating-sum-cancellation argument the directive feared having
387	to reproduce is NOT needed**: `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` encapsulates
388	it. The project work is the *bookkeeping* of mapping the localized Čech
389	complex to a Čech complex over `D(f)` (Branch A step 2), not the cancellation
390	itself.
391	
392	**Naming alignment**: all paths and names cited by the project (iter-047
393	import, iter-053–058 wrappers) remain valid. Recommend the iter-059
394	plan-agent update the iter-056 docstring at
395	`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean:1441` to say
396	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (or `exact_of_localized_span`, depending on
397	which form the project ends up calling — both exist).
398	
399	## Caveats
400	
401	- **Op direction**: Mathlib's `extraDegeneracyCech` builds on
402	  `SimplicialObject.Augmented`, giving an augmented *simplicial* object
403	  with extra degeneracy. The corresponding alternating face map complex
404	  is a *chain* complex. Our Čech complex is a *cochain* complex (built
405	  from an *augmented cosimplicial* object via
406	  `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`). The op-duality is straightforward but
407	  takes ~20-30 LOC of routine repackaging.
408	- **Augmentation construction**: the augmentation `F(U) → ∏_i F(𝒰 i)` is
409	  natural in `F`, so it should be derivable from the universal property
410	  of the formal-coproduct construction. But the project will need to
411	  spell this out — Mathlib does not currently package the augmentation
412	  alongside `cechComplexFunctor`.
413	- **Quasi-coherence assumption**: Stacks 01ED is for *quasi-coherent*
414	  sheaves on affine schemes, where the augmented Čech complex is exact
415	  *as a complex of `Γ(U)`-modules*. Our `toModuleKSheaf` is not the
416	  structure sheaf as an `O_C`-module — it's the structure sheaf as a
417	  *constant `k`-module*. The Stacks proof carries over because the
418	  alternating-sum cancellation is purely *combinatorial* (lives at the
419	  level of the simplicial structure, not the algebra structure). But the
420	  blueprint should note this distinction so future readers don't
421	  conflate "quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_C`-modules" with "sheaves of
422	  `k`-modules underlying the structure sheaf".

721	For `f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X` (with all intersections
722	affine, e.g. `X` separated) and a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`, the *relative Čech
723	complex* `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the cochain complex of `O_S`-modules with degree-`p` term
724	```
725	  Čᵖ(𝒰, F) = ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (f|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}}),
726	```
727	and differential the alternating sum of the restriction maps
728	`(d s)_{i₀…i_{p+1}} = Σⱼ (-1)ʲ s_{i₀…î_j…i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i₀…i_{p+1}}}`. Over an
729	affine `U = Spec A` with `F|_U = M~` and a standard cover by the `D(fᵢ)`, this is
730	the complex of localisations `∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}f_{i₁}} → ⋯`. Each term
731	is quasi-coherent because the intersections are affine and the pushforward of a
732	quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism is
733	quasi-coherent.

/-! ### Dependent-coefficient port (planner **L3 port**)

The localised complex that `exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner **L2**) consumes
node-by-node has *varying* coefficients `M_{s_σ}` — the away localisation of `M`
at the product `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}` — not a single constant module `M`.  After
localising the whole extended complex at a spanning element `s_r`, the index `r`
is globally available and `s_r` becomes a unit, so the prepend
`σ ↦ Fin.cons r σ` is an *isomorphism* on the coefficients:
`M_{s_{cons r σ}} = M_{s_r · s_σ} ≅ M_{s_σ}` because `s_r` is already invertible.

This section ports the constant-coefficient cancellation
(`combHomotopy_spec`, `combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle`) to that dependent
setting.  The away-localisation restriction maps are abstracted as additive
coface maps `δ` (`M_{s_{σ∘dⱼ}} → M_{s_σ}`, well defined because `s_σ` carries the
extra factor `s_{σ j}`) and the prepend isomorphisms as additive maps `c`
(`M_{s_{cons r σ}} → M_{s_σ}`).  The two compatibility identities below are
exactly the ones the away-localisation maps satisfy:

* the **unit** identity `c ∘ δ₀ = id` (deleting the prepended `r` from `cons r σ`
  recovers `σ`; on localisations the composite restriction is the identity since
  `s_r` is a unit), and
* the **shift** identity `c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c` (prepend commutes with the later
  cofaces), which is `cons_comp_succAbove_succ` lifted to the localisation maps.

Threading the dependent family through the same alternating-sum cancellation as
the constant case then yields the dependent homotopy identity and the
cocycle⟹coboundary corollary that L2 consumes.  Constructing the actual `δ`/`c`
from `IsLocalizedModule.Away` (Mathlib `tilde` API:
`AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde`, `IsLocalizedModule (.powers f)`) and the L1
identification of the abstract `CechComplex` terms with `∏_σ M_{s_σ}` remain the
outstanding bridge (see `CechAcyclic.affine`). -/

section Dependent

variable {A : (m : ℕ) → (Fin m → ι) → Type*} [∀ m σ, AddCommGroup (A m σ)]
variable (r : ι)
variable (δ : (m : ℕ) → (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) → (j : Fin (m + 1)) →
    A m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →+ A (m + 1) σ)
variable (c : (m : ℕ) → (σ : Fin m → ι) → A (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) →+ A m σ)

/-- Transport of a dependent cochain value along an equality of index tuples.
The transport friction that the constant-coefficient proof avoided (there every
coefficient is the same `M`) is isolated here. -/
private lemma depTransport {m : ℕ} {x y : Fin m → ι} (h : x = y)
    (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) : h ▸ t x = t y := by
  subst h; rfl

/-- Deleting the prepended index `r` (the `0`-th coface of `Fin.cons r σ`)
recovers `σ`. -/
private lemma cons_comp_zero_succAbove {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) :
    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 1)).succAbove = σ := by
  funext i; simp

/-- Dependent (varying-coefficient) alternating Čech differential built from the
coface (localisation restriction) maps `δ`. -/
private def depDiff {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) :
    ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ :=
  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (m + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • δ m σ j (t (σ ∘ j.succAbove))

/-- Dependent contracting homotopy: prepend the distinguished index `r`, then
apply the prepend map `c`. -/
private def depHomotopy {m : ℕ} (u : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ) :
    ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ :=
  fun σ => c m σ (u (Fin.cons r σ))

/-- **Dependent contracting-homotopy identity** (planner L3 port): `d ∘ h + h ∘ d
= id`, evaluated at a tuple `σ`.  Same alternating-sum cancellation as
`combHomotopy_spec`, threaded through the varying coefficients via the unit
identity `hu` (`c ∘ δ₀ = id`) and the shift identity `hsh`
(`c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c`). -/
private lemma depHomotopy_spec
    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
        (y : A (m + 1)
          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
        (y : A (m + 1)
          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
    depDiff δ (depHomotopy r c t) σ + depHomotopy r c (depDiff δ t) σ = t σ := by
  simp only [depDiff, depHomotopy]
  rw [map_sum]
  simp only [map_zsmul]
  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun k : Fin (m + 2) =>
    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (k : ℕ) • c (m + 1) σ
      (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) k (t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ k.succAbove))))]
  -- the `k = 0` term collapses to `t σ` via the unit identity
  rw [hu σ (t _)]
  rw [depTransport (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) t]
  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
  -- the `k = j.succ` terms cancel against the `d ∘ h` sum in pairs of opposite sign
  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
    rw [hsh σ x (t _), depTransport (cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ x) t,
      pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]
    abel), add_zero]

/-- Dependent cocycle⟹coboundary (the geometric half consumed by L2): if
`depDiff t = 0` then `t = depDiff (depHomotopy t)`. -/
private lemma depDiff_eq_of_cocycle
    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
        (y : A (m + 1)
          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
        (y : A (m + 1)
          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ)
    (ht : depDiff δ t = 0) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
    depDiff δ (depHomotopy r c t) σ = t σ := by
  have h := depHomotopy_spec r δ c hu hsh t σ
  rw [show depHomotopy r c (depDiff δ t) σ = 0 by rw [ht]; rfl, add_zero] at h
  exact h

end Dependent

end CombinatorialCech

/-- The composite coface tuple is symmetric under the `d²=0` index swap
`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` (dependent analogue of the `harg` step
inside `combDifferential_comp`). -/
private lemma comp_succAbove_swap {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι)
    (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1)) :
    (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
      = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
  funext k
  simp only [Function.comp_apply]
  rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]

/-- **Dependent `d² = 0`** for the varying-coefficient alternating Čech complex.
Same sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` as
`combDifferential_comp`, with the coefficient transport handled by
`comp_succAbove_swap` and the coface-commutation hypothesis `hcomm` (the two
restriction maps into `M_{s_σ}` agree — a formal property of localisation maps,
independent of the choice of `r`). -/
private lemma depDiff_comp
    (hcomm : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1))
        (z : A m ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove)),
        δ (m + 1) σ j (δ m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i z)
          = δ (m + 1) σ (j.succAbove i)
              (δ m (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) (i.predAbove j)
                ((comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm ▸ z)))
    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) :
    depDiff δ (depDiff δ t) = 0 := by
  funext σ
  simp only [depDiff, Pi.zero_apply, map_sum, map_zsmul, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (m + 2) × Fin (m + 1) =>
    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) •
      δ (m + 1) σ p.1 (δ m (σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) p.2 (t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))))]
  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
    rw [← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
    rw [hcomm σ j i, depTransport (comp_succAbove_swap σ j i) t]
    simp
  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
    intro hj
    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _

end Dependent

/-- **Dependent positive-degree exactness** in the `Function.Exact` form that
`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner **L2**) consumes after localising at a
spanning element `s_r`.  Combines `depDiff_comp` (`im ⊆ ker`) with
`depDiff_eq_of_cocycle` (`ker ⊆ im`, the homotopy half carrying the
`s_r`-invertibility input).  This is the dependent-coefficient analogue of
`combDifferential_exact`. -/
private lemma depDiff_exact
    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
        (y : A (m + 1)
          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
        (y : A (m + 1)
          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
    (hcomm : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1))
        (z : A m ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove)),
        δ (m + 1) σ j (δ m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i z)
          = δ (m + 1) σ (j.succAbove i)
              (δ m (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) (i.predAbove j)
                ((comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm ▸ z)))
    (m : ℕ) :
    Function.Exact (depDiff δ (m := m + 1)) (depDiff δ (m := m + 2)) := by
  intro x
  constructor
  · intro hx
    exact ⟨depHomotopy r c x,
      funext fun σ => depDiff_eq_of_cocycle r δ c hu hsh x hx σ⟩
  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
    exact depDiff_comp δ hcomm y

end Dependent

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAcyclic.lean

## CechAcyclic.affine (line 74) — assigned sorry

### Status: PARTIAL (sorry remains; substantive reusable infrastructure added)

### Attempt 1 — dependent-coefficient L3 port (RESOLVED, the planned forward step)
- **Approach:** The plan/task_pending explicitly flagged that the constant-`M`
  combinatorial core (`combDifferential_exact`, done iter-015) is only the
  *cancellation skeleton*; the localised complex that L2
  (`exact_of_isLocalized_span`) actually consumes has **varying** coefficients
  `M_{s_σ}` (away localisation at `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}`), needing "a per-tuple family
  with a prepend-iso `A(cons r τ) ≅ A τ`". I built exactly that port.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — added a complete, axiom-clean `CombinatorialCech.Dependent`
  section (only kernel axioms `propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound`, verified
  via `lean_verify` on `depDiff_exact`). New declarations (all compile, no `sorry`):
  - `depTransport` — transport of a dependent cochain value along an index-tuple
    equality (`subst; rfl`); isolates the transport friction the constant proof
    avoided.
  - `cons_comp_zero_succAbove`, `comp_succAbove_swap` — the two index identities
    (`(cons r σ)∘d₀ = σ`; the `d²=0` swap composite symmetry).
  - `depDiff` — dependent alternating Čech differential from abstract coface maps
    `δ : A m (σ∘j.succAbove) →+ A (m+1) σ`.
  - `depHomotopy` — prepend-`r` contracting homotopy from prepend maps
    `c : A (m+1) (cons r σ) →+ A m σ`.
  - `depHomotopy_spec` — **`d∘h + h∘d = id`** (dependent), given the unit identity
    `hu : c ∘ δ₀ = id` and shift identity `hsh : c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c`. Same
    alternating-sum cancellation as `combHomotopy_spec`, threaded through the
    varying coefficients.
  - `depDiff_eq_of_cocycle` — cocycle ⟹ coboundary (the geometric half).
  - `depDiff_comp` — **dependent `d²=0`**, via the `(j,i)↦(j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)`
    sign-reversing involution + coface-commutation `hcomm` (formal localisation-map
    property).
  - `depDiff_exact` — **`Function.Exact (depDiff δ) (depDiff δ)`**, the exact shape
    L2 (`exact_of_isLocalized_span`) consumes per node after localising at `s_r`.
- **Key insight:** Abstracting the away-localisation restriction maps as additive
  `δ`/`c` with three compatibility hypotheses (`hu`/`hsh`/`hcomm`) keeps the
  combinatorial cancellation completely decoupled from localisation theory and from
  the transport pain. The transports `(cons r σ)∘0.succAbove = σ` and
  `cons_comp_succAbove_succ` are discharged by `depTransport` (a one-line `subst`).

### Remaining blocker — L1 categorical→module bridge (NOT closed; missing infrastructure)
The top-level `CechAcyclic.affine` `sorry` stays. To close it one must:
1. **Reduce** `IsZero ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology p)` (a homology object in
   `S.Modules = SheafOfModules`) to a `Function.Exact` statement about `R`-modules.
2. **Identify** the abstract complex terms — `CechComplex f 𝒰 F =
   relativeCechComplexOfNerve f (CechNerve 𝒰 F)`, i.e. degree-`p` term is
   `pushforward f` applied to `pushPullObj F (D(s_σ) ↪ Spec R) = (incl)_* (incl)^* F`
   — with the concrete localised modules `∏_σ M_{s_σ}`, AND the abstract
   cosimplicial differential with the alternating localisation coboundary.
3. **Construct** the concrete `δ`/`c` maps and discharge `hu`/`hsh`/`hcomm`, then
   feed `depDiff_exact` per node through `exact_of_isLocalized_span (Set.range s) hs`.

Step 2 is the deep gap. Relevant Mathlib found: `AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde`
gives `instance (f : R) : IsLocalizedModule (.powers f) (tilde.toOpen M (basicOpen f)).hom`
(sections of the tilde sheaf over `D(f)` = away localisation) and
`isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen`. But connecting an arbitrary quasicoherent
`F : (Spec R).Modules` to `tilde M`, computing sections of `pushPullObj F`, the
pushforward along `f`, and the cosimplicial→cochain differential — none of this
project-local infrastructure exists. The analogy `cech-koszul-precedent.md` (a
different project's design) estimates ~150–250 LOC just for the section-localisation
bridge in a much simpler (constant-`k`-module) setting; here it is the relative,
quasicoherent, `Scheme.Modules` setting.

### Why no informal-agent call
No usable LLM API key in env (`env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"`
shows only `GEMINI_CLI_*` IDE-server tokens, not `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — consistent with
the standing PROGRESS.md note. `archon-informal-agent.py` unavailable.

## Suggested next step for the planner
The L1 bridge is genuinely a separate **mathlib-build / new-file** lane, not a body
fill on this file. It needs, bottom-up:
1. `(pushPullObj F (D(s_σ) ↪ Spec R))` sections over basic opens identified with
   `IsLocalizedModule.Away` of `Γ(Spec R, F)` (via `tilde`/quasicoherence).
2. The `CechNerve`/`relativeCechComplexOfNerve` differential identified with the
   alternating localisation coboundary (= `depDiff` for the concrete `δ`).
3. `IsZero (homology p) ↔ Function.Exact d^{p-1} d^p` for cochain complexes in
   `S.Modules` (likely via `HomologicalComplex` + section-wise exactness on an
   affine base, or `exact_of_isLocalized_span` after the module identification).
Once that lane lands, `CechAcyclic.affine` is a short assembly:
`exact_of_isLocalized_span` + `CombinatorialCech.Dependent.depDiff_exact`.

## Summary
- **sorry count:** 1 → 1 (the assigned `CechAcyclic.affine` remains open).
- **Closed:** none of the pre-existing sorries (only the one, which is L1-blocked).
- **Added (genuine compiling code, axiom-clean):** 8 new `CombinatorialCech.Dependent`
  declarations (`depTransport`, `cons_comp_zero_succAbove`, `comp_succAbove_swap`,
  `depDiff`, `depHomotopy`, `depHomotopy_spec`, `depDiff_eq_of_cocycle`,
  `depDiff_comp`, `depDiff_exact`) — the full dependent-coefficient L3 port the plan
  requested. No bare sorries introduced; the dependent section has zero sorries and
  zero warnings.
- **Open & why:** `CechAcyclic.affine` — blocked on the L1 categorical→module bridge
  (missing sheaf-section infrastructure, detailed above). Both L3 forms (constant +
  dependent) and the L2 certifier are now in place; only L1 remains.
- **Adjacent sorries:** none other in this file.

## Blueprint marker note (for review agent)
The new dependent declarations are `private` helpers for `lem:cech_acyclic_affine`.
Per the bundling convention they should be appended to the existing multi-name
`\lean{...}` list of `lem:cech_acyclic_affine` (alongside the `CombinatorialCech.*`
constant-coefficient bundle): `AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.Dependent.depDiff`,
`...depHomotopy`, `...depTransport`, `...cons_comp_zero_succAbove`,
`...comp_succAbove_swap`, `...depHomotopy_spec`, `...depDiff_eq_of_cocycle`,
`...depDiff_comp`, `...depDiff_exact`. (Provers cannot edit the blueprint.)

## Why I stopped
`Partial progress`: added 9 axiom-clean, compiling declarations forming the complete
**dependent-coefficient L3 port** (`CombinatorialCech.Dependent.*`, culminating in
`depDiff_exact : Function.Exact …`) — the specific forward step the plan flagged as
needed ("per-tuple family with a prepend-iso `A(cons r τ) ≅ A τ`"). This is genuine
Lean code, not comments. The assigned `CechAcyclic.affine` sorry is NOT closed: it is
blocked on the L1 categorical→module bridge, which requires new sheaf-section
infrastructure (sections of `pushPullObj` over basic opens = localised modules; the
abstract cosimplicial differential = alternating localisation coboundary; `IsZero
homology ↔ Function.Exact`). That bridge is a separate mathlib-build lane, not a body
fill on this file, and cannot be stubbed without weakening the type. The informal
agent was unavailable (no API key). I attempted no half-baked L1 stub because every
honest route to L1 requires the missing infrastructure first; leaving the typed
`sorry` with the now-complete L2/L3 scaffolding below it is the correct partial state.

(D(s_σ)↪Spec R))` with `∏_σ M_{s_σ}` (away-localisations) and the cosimplicial
differential with the alternating localisation coboundary, (b) construct the

## Summary

**Assigned task:** close the `sorry` in `CechAcyclic.affine` (standard-cover Čech vanishing on affines).

**Outcome: Partial progress** — sorry count 1 → 1. The assigned sorry is **not** closed (it's blocked on missing infrastructure, detailed below), but I added the complete, axiom-clean **dependent-coefficient L3 port** the plan explicitly requested.

### What I built (9 new declarations, all compiling, axiom-clean)
New sub-namespace `CombinatorialCech.Dependent` — the varying-coefficient (`M_{s_σ}`) port of the constant-`M` core:
- `depTransport`, `cons_comp_zero_succAbove`, `comp_succAbove_swap` — index/transport helpers
- `depDiff`, `depHomotopy` — dependent alternating differential + prepend-`r` homotopy from abstract additive coface maps `δ` and prepend maps `c`
- `depHomotopy_spec` — `d∘h + h∘d = id` (given unit `hu` + shift `hsh` compatibilities)
- `depDiff_eq_of_cocycle` — cocycle ⟹ coboundary (geometric half)
- `depDiff_comp` — dependent `d²=0` (sign-reversing involution + commutation `hcomm`)
- `depDiff_exact` — **`Function.Exact`**, the exact shape `exact_of_isLocalized_span` (L2) consumes per node. Verified via `lean_verify`: only kernel axioms.

This was the planned forward step ("per-tuple family with a prepend-iso `A(cons r τ) ≅ A τ`"). With it, **both L3 forms (constant + dependent) and the L2 certifier are now ready** — only L1 remains.

### Why the top-level sorry stays — L1 categorical→module bridge (missing infrastructure)
Closing `CechAcyclic.affine` requires reducing `IsZero ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology p)` (homology in `SheafOfModules`) to `Function.Exact` of `R`-modules, by identifying the abstract `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`/`pushPullObj`/pushforward construction with the concrete `∏_σ M_{s_σ}` localized complex, then constructing concrete `δ`/`c` and discharging `hu`/`hsh`/`hcomm`. Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde` supplies the section-localization fact (`IsLocalizedModule (.powers f) (tilde.toOpen M (basicOpen f)).hom`), but **the project-local sheaf-section / pushforward / cosimplicial-differential infrastructure does not exist** — this is a separate mathlib-build/new-file lane, not a body fill on this file. I did not stub L1 (would require weakening the type). Informal agent unavailable (no API key; only `GEMINI_CLI_*` IDE tokens present).

Task result written to `task_results/CechAcyclic.lean.md`; memory updated to record the dependent port as done and L1 as the sole remaining blocker.

You are a prover agent for project 'Cech-Cohomology'. Current stage: prover.
Archon iteration: 016.
Project directory: /home/archon/proj/Cech-Cohomology
Project state directory: /home/archon/proj/Cech-Cohomology/.archon
Read /home/archon/proj/Cech-Cohomology/.archon/CLAUDE.md for your role, then read /home/archon/proj/Cech-Cohomology/.archon/PROGRESS.md.
Check your .lean file for /- USER: ... -/ comments for file-specific hints.

IMPORTANT:
- You own ONLY the file assigned below. Do NOT edit any other .lean file.
- Write your results to /home/archon/proj/Cech-Cohomology/.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md when done.
- Do NOT edit PROGRESS.md, task_pending.md, or task_done.md.
- Missing Mathlib infrastructure is NEVER a valid reason to leave a sorry.
- NEVER revert to a bare sorry. Always leave your partial proof attempt in the code.

Blueprint chapter for your file: blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex
- Read it BEFORE writing any Lean code — it contains the informal proof
  written by the plan agent.
- After you formalize a declaration, mark its blueprint environment with \leanok.
- If the chapter file does not yet exist, create it with a minimal \chapter block
  and note in your task_results that the plan agent should flesh it out.

        ## Archon memory (read only — do NOT modify this file)

        ```
        - leandag graphs **statement-level `\uses{}` ONLY**, not proof-block `\uses`. To wire a dep into the goal cone, put it in the STATEMENT `\uses` (mirror it from the proof, as the goal node does). Proof-only `\uses` ⇒ severed cone + isolated nodes.
- Cover an internal Lean helper by appending its name to an existing related declaration's multi-name `\lean{...}` list (bundling pattern), not by adding a new environment. `private` does NOT exempt a helper from `unmatched`/coverage debt — bundle it too.
        ```

## Protected by the mathematician (`archon-protected.yaml`)

The mathematician owns everything below; respect every rule. Do NOT set or accept an objective that requires violating one.

**Frozen signatures** (you MAY fill the proof body / close a `sorry`, but never rename / re-type / reorder args / weaken hypotheses):
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean`: `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage`


## Blueprint dependency graph (leandag)

You can navigate the project's dependency graph read-only to find the
proven lemmas/defs your goal can lean on (with the exact `\lean{}`
names to apply) instead of re-deriving them. `archon` is on PATH;
`--json` prints parseable JSON to stdout (banner to stderr):

```
archon dag-query ancestors --node <blueprint-label-of-your-target>   # proven deps available to you
archon dag-query node      --node <label>                            # one declaration's status
```

It is a navigation aid only — the proof obligation and your blueprint
chapter remain the source of truth.

Active prover mode: **mathlib-build**

## Your goal

Build project-local Mathlib infrastructure axiom-clean, one step at a time, going **as far as possible** in this iteration. Your output consists of definitions and lemmas that compile without `sorry` and whose `#print axioms` shows only `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. You stop only when you hit a genuine mathematical blocker — not because one step is hard.

## Invariant

**No `sorry` in your output.** Every declaration you add must be fully proved. If you cannot close the current step: try alternatives first, then either prove a smaller sub-step or leave the declaration absent. Never add a declaration with `sorry`.

## Workflow

### 1. Orient

- Read `PROGRESS.md` for the objective: what infrastructure is needed, and why.
- Read the relevant blueprint chapter. It often contains the mathematical path to the ingredient you're building.
- Read `task_pending.md` for prior dead ends. Do not repeat them.

### 2. Scout the Mathlib API

Before writing any Lean, search for what already exists:

- `lean_local_search` for related names.
- `lean_leansearch` with a description of the mathematical content.
- `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns.
- Look for near-misses: a lemma that covers a sub-case, an instance you can compose, a `simp` set that contains what you need.

Even if you think you know the API — search anyway. Signatures change, instances disappear.

### 3. Build bottom-up, step by step

Start from the deepest missing piece identified in your scouting. For each step:

1. **Draft the type signature** and check it typechecks via `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lean_run_code` before attempting the body.
2. **Write the proof.** Use Mathlib lemmas you verified exist. If a sub-step is missing, recurse: prove that sub-step first.
3. **Verify axiom-cleanliness** immediately after the step compiles: `#print axioms MyLemma`. Any `sorryAx` in the ancestry means the step is not axiom-clean — do not proceed until it is gone.
4. **Continue to the next step** in the chain.

Push as far as you can. Each axiom-clean step you add is permanent progress.

### 4. When stuck on a step — try before stopping

Before declaring a step impossible:

1. **Try a different proof route.** Reformulate the statement, weaken to a special case you can prove, use a detour through an equivalent form.
2. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 3.
3. **Search more broadly.** The lemma you need might exist under a different name or in a namespace you haven't checked.
4. **Prove a strictly smaller sub-step** that is axiom-clean and genuinely useful — something that shrinks the remaining gap.

Only after exhausting these alternatives do you stop. **Comments describing an approach you haven't tried are not exhausted alternatives — attempt them first.**

### 5. Stopping

**Before stopping, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote in a comment, TODO, or log entry? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there adjacent declarations in the chain I could attempt with the infrastructure I just built?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before declaring the step impossible?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped? Attempt it — a failed attempt with a named error is better than a comment.

Stop only when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable." **Writing a comment about a possible approach counts as identifying it — which means you should attempt it.**

**You are fully authorized to make every mathematical and structural decision within your assigned file without waiting for the planner.** "I'll let the planner decide which route to take" is never a valid stop reason — pick the most promising route, attempt it, document the outcome. The planner corrects route choices next iter; an idle session corrects nothing.

Stop when you have tried alternatives and cannot make further axiom-clean progress. Before stopping:

- Commit all axiom-clean steps you completed.
- Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with a precise handoff (see Logging below).
- Ensure the file compiles with no `sorry`.

## File structure

Place new declarations under a clearly delimited section:

```lean
/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — <TopicName> -/
```

- Use `private` for helpers with no downstream use outside this file.
- Use `theorem` / `lemma` (non-private) for steps that other files will import.
- Add a one-line docstring to each non-private declaration explaining why it is project-local.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any existing declaration. You may add new declarations freely. You must not rename, re-type, or modify signatures of protected declarations.

## API alignment

Mathlib names and signatures are the most common failure source.

- Verify every name with `lean_local_search` or `lean_loogle` — do not rely on memory.
- Check that typeclass assumptions in your statement match what Mathlib requires.
- For `def`-backed types: Lean may not unfold them for typeclass synthesis; use explicit coercions or `change` / `show` in the proof body.

## Write permissions

Only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror the `.lean` path):

```markdown
# Algebra/MathlibSupplement.lean

## Session summary
- Built axiom-clean: `Foo.bar`, `Foo.baz` (lines 12–40)
- Blocked on: `Foo.qux` — needs `Bar.aux` which requires [precise statement]

## Foo.bar (line 12)
- **Approach:** Direct from `Mathlib.Algebra.X` via `simp [Y]`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean

## Foo.qux (not added)
- **Approach 1:** Tried [route] — FAILED because [reason]
- **Approach 2:** Tried informal agent — suggested [sketch], formalization blocked on [specific gap]
- **Next step:** Build `Bar.aux : [precise type statement]` first
- **Dead end:** Do not retry [route] — [why it fails]
```

One section per declaration attempted. For each: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / NOT ADDED), dead-end warnings, next step.

## End-of-session handoff

**Write a `## Summary` section** before the verdicts: declarations added (count + names), declarations blocked (count + why), sorry count before → after across your file.

**Write a `## Needs blueprint entry` section.** This mode is the biggest source of blueprint debt: every non-private definition/lemma you add is, by construction, infrastructure with **no blueprint block yet** — a `lean_aux` node invisible to the dependency graph (`archon dag-query unmatched` will list it) until the planner/reviewer blueprints it. List each new non-private declaration (name, file, and the facts its proof relies on) so the 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence the dag agent built is restored next iter. Closing 0 project sorries while adding 3 unreported helpers does not advance the project — it quietly re-introduces the isolation the DAG phase worked to eliminate. Reporting them is mandatory.

**Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest. Only axiom-clean Lean declarations count as progress. Comments, sketches, and log prose do NOT. If you wrote down approaches you didn't attempt, say so:

- `Real progress`: N axiom-clean declarations added — name each one with its line number.
- `Partial progress`: added some declarations, hit a specific blocker on the next — name the blocker precisely (not "it's complex", but the exact type or missing ingredient).
- `Approaches written but not attempted`: identified routes in comments or log but did not attempt them — name each. This is valid but weak; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
- `Blocked — alternatives exhausted`: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), alternative routes tried, all failed — name what was tried, why each failed, and the exact type statement of the next needed ingredient.
- `Infrastructure already exists`: Mathlib has what was needed — cite the exact lemma name and namespace.
