1	\chapter{{\v C}ech computation of higher direct images $R^i f_*$ (unconditional)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean
4	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAcyclic.lean
5	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/PresheafCech.lean
6	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FreePresheafComplex.lean
7	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
8	% quasi-coherent sheaves and \S Quasi-coherence of higher direct images
9	% (Lemmas \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial},
10	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero},
11	% \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent},
12	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}) (read from
13	% references/stacks-coherent.tex).
14	
15	\section{Setup and motivation}
16	
17	This chapter constructs the higher derived direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) for
18	\(i \geq 1\) \emph{without appealing to injective resolutions in the category of
19	sheaves of modules}. The companion development of
20	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage} defines \(R^i f_*\) as a right
21	derived functor via injective resolutions, which requires the ambient category of
22	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to have enough injectives. The {\v C}ech approach
23	developed here sidesteps this requirement:
24	it computes \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as the cohomology of an explicit complex built
25	from the pushforwards of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the finite intersections of an affine
26	open cover, and so produces an \emph{unconditional} construction of \(R^i f_*\) for
27	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) and separated quasi-compact \(f\).
28	
29	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
30	separated (so that all finite intersections of an affine open cover of \(X\) are
31	again affine), and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. We
32	write \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) for the category of quasi-coherent
33	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. A base change of \(f\) along a morphism \(g : S' \to S\)
34	is recorded by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
35	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
36	\[
37	  \begin{array}{ccc}
38	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
39	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
40	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
41	  \end{array}
42	\]
43	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), \(g' : X' \to X\) is the first
44	projection, and \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the pullback of
45	\(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
46	
47	\section{The {\v C}ech nerve and {\v C}ech complex of an affine cover}
48	
49	\begin{definition}
50	
51	\leanok
52	[{\v C}ech nerve of an affine open cover]
53	  \label{def:cech_nerve}
54	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve}
55	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:push_pull_functor, def:cech_nerve_cosimplicial, lem:push_pull_id, lem:push_pull_comp}
56	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
57	  % quasi-coherent sheaves, opening paragraph and Lemma
58	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}
59	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L36--67).
60	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
61	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an affine open.
62	  % Recall that a {\it standard open covering} of $U$ is a covering
63	  % of the form $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$
64	  % where $f_1, \ldots, f_n \in \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)$ generate
65	  % the unit ideal, see
66	  % Schemes, Definition \ref{schemes-definition-standard-covering}."
67	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
68	  quasi-coherent sheaves.}
69	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
70	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite open cover of \(X\) by
71	  affine opens, with index set \(I\). For a finite nonempty tuple
72	  \(i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I\) write
73	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = U_{i_0} \cap \cdots \cap U_{i_p}\) for the corresponding
74	  intersection. The \emph{{\v C}ech nerve} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the augmented
75	  \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) whose object in
76	  cosimplicial degree \(p\) is the direct image, along the open immersions
77	  \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\), of the restriction
78	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (or of any chosen quasi-coherent sheaf) to the
79	  \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
80	  \[
81	    [p] \;\longmapsto\;
82	      \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
83	        (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_* \,\bigl(\,\cdot\,|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
84	  \]
85	  with cofaces given by the restriction maps that omit one index and
86	  codegeneracies by the maps that repeat one index; the augmentation in degree
87	  \(-1\) is the object on all of \(X\). The variance is essential: the geometric
88	  nerve of the cover (the iterated fibre powers of \(\coprod_i U_i\) over \(X\),
89	  introduced in \S\ref{sec:cech_three_part} below) is a \emph{simplicial} object
90	  in \emph{schemes}, but applying the covariant direct-image functor turns it into
91	  a \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\); consequently the
92	  associated alternating-coface complex is a \emph{cochain} complex (differentials
93	  raising degree), not a chain complex. When \(X\) is separated each intersection
94	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is itself affine. The local affine model is the
95	  \emph{standard open covering} \(U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) of an affine open
96	  \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) by basic opens, where \(f_1, \ldots, f_n \in A\)
97	  generate the unit ideal; on such a cover the intersections are again basic opens
98	  \(D(f_{i_0} \cdots f_{i_p})\).
99	\end{definition}
100	
101	\begin{definition}
102	
103	\leanok
104	[{\v C}ech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf]
105	  \label{def:cech_complex}
106	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex}
107	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, def:relative_cech_complex_of_nerve}
108	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
109	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof)
110	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L59--77).
111	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
112	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
113	  % is identified with the complex
114	  % $$
115	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
116	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
117	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
118	  % \ldots
119	  % $$
120	  % We are asked to show that the extended complex
121	  % \begin{equation}
122	  % \label{equation-extended}
123	  % 0 \to
124	  % M \to
125	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
126	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
127	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
128	  % \ldots
129	  % \end{equation}
130	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
131	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact."
132	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
133	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover {\v C}ech
134	  vanishing).}
135	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
136	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with
137	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) affine (e.g.\ \(X\) separated). The
138	  \emph{{\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
139	  is the cochain complex obtained by applying the global-sections functor to the
140	  {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\);
141	  in degree \(p\) it is
142	  \[
143	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
144	      = \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
145	        \mathcal{F}\bigl(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
146	  \]
147	  with differential \(d : \check{\mathcal{C}}^p \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^{p+1}\) the
148	  alternating sum \((d s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
149	  s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}\) of the
150	  restriction maps. Over an affine \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) with
151	  \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) and a standard cover by the
152	  \(D(f_i)\), this is the complex
153	  \(\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to \cdots\) of
154	  localisations. The \emph{relative {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet
155	  (\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) for \(f : X \to S\) is the analogous complex in
156	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) obtained by applying \(f_*\) over each finite
157	  intersection instead of global sections; its terms are
158	  \(\prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_*
159	  \bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr)\), and it is a complex in
160	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) because each \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine and the
161	  pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated
162	  morphism is quasi-coherent.
163	\end{definition}
164	
165	\subsection{The three-part construction of the relative {\v C}ech complex}
166	\label{sec:cech_three_part}
167	
168	It is convenient to factor the construction of
169	Definitions~\ref{def:cech_nerve}--\ref{def:cech_complex} into three independent
170	pieces. Two of them are purely formal and unconditional; the sole non-trivial
171	mathematical content is isolated in the middle piece.
172	
173	\paragraph{(1) Geometric backbone — the augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow.}
174	Package the affine cover as a single morphism
175	\(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) (the canonical map out of the
176	coproduct induced by the inclusions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\)). The augmented
177	{\v C}ech nerve of this arrow is an augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
178	\(X\): its object in simplicial degree \(p\) is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
179	\[
180	  \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
181	    \;=\;
182	    \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
183	      U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
184	\]
185	with the augmentation the cover map to \(X\). Because the \(U_i\) are open
186	subschemes of \(X\), each fibre product \(U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p}\)
187	is canonically the intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). This backbone exists
188	\emph{unconditionally}: it uses only that the category of schemes has coproducts
189	and finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the {\v C}ech nerve of an
190	arrow). It carries no sheaf-theoretic data yet — it is the simplicial diagram of
191	spaces over which the construction is indexed.
192	
193	\paragraph{(2) Push--pull functor — lifting the backbone to modules.}
194	The geometric backbone is turned into a cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module by
195	the \emph{relative-direct-image functor on the over-category}
196	\[
197	  G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
198	  \qquad
199	  (Y \xrightarrow{\,p\,} X) \;\longmapsto\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
200	\]
201	sending an \(X\)-scheme \(p : Y \to X\) to the pushforward along \(p\) of the
202	pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\). Composing \(G\) with the (opposite of the) simplicial
203	backbone of (1) produces exactly the augmented cosimplicial object of
204	Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: on the degree-\(p\) piece
205	\(p : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\) one has
206	\(p_* p^*\mathcal{F} = (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\),
207	and a morphism of \(X\)-schemes \(h : (Y_1, p_1) \to (Y_2, p_2)\) (so
208	\(h \circ p_2 = p_1\)\,, reading \(p\) as the structure map) induces the
209	restriction comparison \(G(p_2) \to G(p_1)\) via the unit/counit of the
210	pull-push adjunction together with the over-triangle identifying the two
211	structure maps. This functor \(G\) is the \emph{one non-formal step} in the whole
212	construction. Its functoriality --- that \(G\) respects identities and composition
213	of morphisms over \(X\) --- is precisely a statement about the coherence of the
214	comparison isomorphisms \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) and
215	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\) for pushforward and pullback of sheaves of
216	modules, together with their compatibility with the adjunction units. Concretely,
217	pullback and pushforward of sheaves of modules are organised as a
218	pseudofunctor \(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to
219	\mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\) (sending a morphism to the adjoint pair
220	\(p^* \dashv p_*\)), and the comparison isomorphisms
221	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\), \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) are its structure
222	\(2\)-cells. The functoriality of \(G\) needs exactly three facts: the mate identity
223	expressing the conjugate of the inverse pullback comparison as the pushforward
224	comparison, its normalisation at the identity morphism, and the pseudofunctor
225	unitor and pentagon (associativity) coherences. The laws are nonetheless not formal
226	trivialities: each is a multi-step mate calculation that transports the comparison
227	isomorphisms along the over-triangle \(g \circ p_2 = p_1\) of structure maps and
228	past the adjunction unit, transporting along the over-triangle equality; the
229	obstacle is the bookkeeping of those transports, not a missing geometric ingredient.
230	
231	\paragraph{(3) Coherence-free plumbing — from the nerve to the cochain complex.}
232	The passage from the augmented cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module of (1)--(2)
233	to the relative {\v C}ech complex in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) is again purely
234	formal and uses \emph{no} pushforward/pullback coherence. One forgets the
235	augmentation, pushes the cosimplicial object forward along \(f : X \to S\) (a
236	covariant operation applied degreewise), and takes the alternating-coface-map
237	cochain complex of the resulting cosimplicial object of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules.
238	This step relies only on the preadditivity of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\): the
239	alternating sum \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j d^j\) of the cofaces is defined in any
240	preadditive category and squares to zero by the cosimplicial identities. Thus the
241	relative {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
242	of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} is genuinely \emph{defined} in terms of the
243	{\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: once the nerve is constructed,
244	the complex follows with no further input. In summary, of the three pieces only
245	the push--pull functor (2) requires content beyond formal category theory, and
246	that content is the pseudofunctor coherence of pushforward and pullback: the
247	comparison \(2\)-cells of the pseudofunctor
248	\(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to \mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\)
249	and their unitor/pentagon identities.
250	
251	\paragraph{Formal bricks of the three-part construction.}
252	The geometric backbone (1), the push--pull functor (2), and the plumbing (3) are
253	recorded as the following declarations. The backbone and plumbing are
254	unconditional; the push--pull object and morphism maps are likewise unconditional
255	bricks, and the functor laws assembling them are the lone coherence statement.
256	
257	\begin{definition}\leanok
258	[Arrow of an affine cover]
259	  \label{def:cover_arrow}
260	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrow}
261	  Let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be an affine open cover. Its
262	  \emph{cover arrow} is the single morphism
263	  \(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) obtained as the universal map out of
264	  the coproduct induced by the open immersions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\). Packaging
265	  the cover as one arrow lets the generic {\v C}ech-nerve-of-an-arrow machinery
266	  apply.
267	\end{definition}
268	
269	\begin{definition}[Augmented {\v C}ech nerve of a cover]
270	  \label{def:cover_cech_nerve}
271	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerve, AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerveOver, AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerveOverAug}
272	  \uses{def:cover_arrow}
273	  The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of the cover arrow of
274	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_arrow}: the augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
275	  \(X\) whose degree-\(p\) object is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
276	  \[
277	    \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
278	      = \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
279	        U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
280	  \]
281	  with augmentation the cover map to \(X\). It exists unconditionally because the
282	  category of schemes has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the
283	  {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow). This is the geometric backbone (1).
284	\end{definition}
285	
286	\begin{definition}\leanok
287	[Push--pull object map]
288	  \label{def:push_pull_obj}
289	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullObj}
290	  The object map of the push--pull functor \(G\): to an \(X\)-scheme
291	  \(p : Y \to X\) it assigns
292	  \[
293	    G(Y, p) \;=\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
294	  \]
295	  the pushforward along \(p\) of the pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\).
296	\end{definition}
297	
298	\begin{definition}[Push--pull comparison map]
299	  \label{def:push_pull_map}
300	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_eq_raw, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_self, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_self_gen, AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardComp_hom_app_id, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_unit_comp}
301	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj}
302	  The morphism map of \(G\). A morphism \(g : (Y_2, p_2) \to (Y_1, p_1)\) of
303	  \(X\)-schemes has underlying map \(\bar g : Y_2 \to Y_1\) satisfying the
304	  over-triangle \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\); the contravariant functor produces the
305	  restriction comparison \(G(Y_1, p_1) \to G(Y_2, p_2)\) as the five-step composite
306	  \[
307	    p_{1*}\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
308	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{1*}(\eta)\,}
309	    p_{1*}\,\bar g_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
310	      \xrightarrow{\,\sim\,}
311	    (p_1 \circ \bar g)_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
312	      \;=\;
313	    p_{2*}\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
314	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{2*}(\sim)\,}
315	    p_{2*}\,p_2^*\mathcal{F},
316	  \]
317	  where \(\eta\) is the unit of the adjunction \(\bar g^* \dashv \bar g_*\), the
318	  second arrow is the pushforward comparison \(p_{1*}\bar g_* \cong (p_1 \bar g)_*\),
319	  the equality is the substitution \(p_2 = p_1 \circ \bar g\) of the over-triangle,
320	  and the last arrow applies \(p_{2*}\) to the pullback comparison
321	  \(\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (p_1 \bar g)^* = p_2^*\). The two over-triangle
322	  substitutions are realised as coercions along \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\).
323	\end{definition}
324	
325	\begin{lemma}
326	[Push--pull functor — identity law]
327	  \label{lem:push_pull_id}
328	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_id}
329	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map}
330	  The morphism map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} respects identities:
331	  \(G(\mathrm{id}_Y) = \mathrm{id}_{G(Y)}\) for every \(X\)-scheme \(Y\).
332	\end{lemma}
333	
334	\begin{proof}
335	  A direct calculation with comparison isomorphisms, with no sectionwise content.
336	  Rewrite the head \(p_*(\eta)\) followed by the pushforward comparison as the
337	  conjugate-mate of the inverse pullback comparison; the identity leg then collapses
338	  by the identity normalisation of the pseudofunctor together with right unitality,
339	  and the two over-triangle transport coercions cancel against the unitality coercion.
340	\end{proof}
341	
342	\begin{lemma}
343	[Push--pull functor — composition law]
344	  \label{lem:push_pull_comp}
345	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_comp, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_comp, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_pentagon}
346	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map, lem:push_pull_unit_mate, lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
347	  The morphism map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} is contravariantly functorial:
348	  for composable morphisms \(h : Y_3 \to Y_2\), \(g : Y_2 \to Y_1\) of \(X\)-schemes,
349	  \[
350	    G(g \circ h) = G(h) \circ G(g).
351	  \]
352	  Together with Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_id} this assembles
353	  Definitions~\ref{def:push_pull_obj}--\ref{def:push_pull_map} into a functor
354	  \(G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \to \operatorname{QCoh}(X)\); composing \(G\) with
355	  the backbone of Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} yields the {\v C}ech nerve of
356	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}.
357	\end{lemma}
358	
359	\begin{proof}
360	  \uses{lem:push_pull_unit_mate, lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
361	  The composition identity \(G(g \circ h) = G(h) \circ G(g)\) follows from three
362	  coherence facts. First, the composite adjunction unit \(\eta^{g \circ h}\) decomposes into
363	  the iterated units \(\eta^{g}\), \(\eta^{h}\) via the mate identity of
364	  Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_unit_mate} and the naturality of the adjunction unit. Second,
365	  the resulting pullback-side identity is the \emph{pentagon coherence} of the pullback
366	  pseudofunctor — the associativity \(2\)-cell relating the two bracketings of the comparison
367	  isomorphism \((p \circ q \circ r)^* \cong r^* q^* p^*\). Third, the over-triangle transport
368	  coherences are absorbed by Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}.
369	\end{proof}
370	
371	\begin{lemma}
372	[Base-change unit (mate) identity for the push--pull head]
373	  \label{lem:push_pull_unit_mate}
374	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_unit_mate}
375	  \uses{def:push_pull_map}
376	  For composable scheme morphisms \(f : A \to B\), \(p : B \to Z\) and a
377	  \(\mathcal{O}_Z\)-module \(N\), the adjunction unit \(\eta^p_N\) of
378	  \(p^* \dashv p_*\) at \(N\), followed by \(p_*\) of the unit \(\eta^f\) for
379	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) at \(p^*N\), followed by the pushforward comparison
380	  \(p_* f_* \cong (f \circ p)_*\), equals the unit \(\eta^{f \circ p}_N\) for the
381	  composite followed by \((f \circ p)_*\) applied to the inverse pullback
382	  comparison \((f \circ p)^* \cong f^* p^*\):
383	  \[
384	    \eta^p_N \;\circ\; p_*(\eta^f_{p^*N}) \;\circ\;
385	      \bigl(p_* f_* \cong (f \circ p)_*\bigr)
386	    \;=\;
387	    \eta^{f \circ p}_N \;\circ\; (f \circ p)_*\bigl((f\circ p)^* \cong f^* p^*\bigr)^{-1}.
388	  \]
389	  This is the mate-calculus core that converts the single-morphism unit
390	  \(\eta^{f \circ p}\) into the iterated units \(\eta^p, \eta^f\); it is the
391	  reusable ingredient consumed by the functoriality (pentagon) law of the
392	  push--pull comparison map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map}.
393	\end{lemma}
394	
395	\begin{proof}
396	  A direct mate-calculus computation: unfold both sides using the definition of
397	  the adjunction unit for the composite and the pseudofunctor structure maps, then
398	  verify the equality by the mate identity for the pushforward/pullback comparison.
399	\end{proof}
400	
401	\begin{lemma}
402	[Over-triangle transport cancellation for the push--pull tail]
403	  \label{lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
404	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_transport_cancel}
405	  \uses{def:push_pull_map}
406	  Let \(\bar g : Y_2 \to Y_1\), \(p_1 : Y_1 \to X\), \(p_2 : Y_2 \to X\) satisfy the
407	  over-triangle \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\), and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a
408	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then the pullback-comparison leg of the push--pull map,
409	  conjugated by the two \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) coercions along the over-triangle,
410	  collapses to the transport-light form
411	  \[
412	    e_1 \;\circ\; p_{2*}\bigl((\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (\bar g p_1)^*)_{\mathcal{F}}\bigr)
413	      \;\circ\; e_2
414	    \;=\;
415	    (\bar g p_1)_*\bigl((\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (\bar g p_1)^*)_{\mathcal{F}}\bigr)
416	      \;\circ\; e_3,
417	  \]
418	  where \(e_1, e_2, e_3\) are the \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) coercions induced by
419	  \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\). It states the over-triangle cancellation in the
420	  push--pull comparison map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} with the
421	  over-triangle equality as a free hypothesis, so a single substitution turns the
422	  transports into identities; it is the reusable pre-coherence brick for the
423	  composition (pentagon) law.
424	\end{lemma}
425	
426	\begin{proof}
427	  Substitute the over-triangle equality \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\) to replace
428	  transport coercions by identities, then verify the cancellation by direct
429	  calculation with the pushforward comparison.
430	\end{proof}
431	
432	\begin{definition}
433	[Push--pull functor]
434	  \label{def:push_pull_functor}
435	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullFunctor}
436	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map, lem:push_pull_id, lem:push_pull_comp}
437	  The \emph{push--pull functor}
438	  \[
439	    G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
440	  \]
441	  assembling the object map \(p \mapsto p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F}\) of
442	  Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_obj} and the comparison morphism map of
443	  Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} into a genuine functor. Functoriality is exactly the two
444	  laws established above: the identity law
445	  Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_id} and the composition law Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_comp}. The
446	  functor is contravariant in the over-category \((X/\mathbf{Sch})\); it is the lift of the
447	  geometric backbone (1) to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described as step (2) above.
448	\end{definition}
449	
450	\begin{definition}
451	[{\v C}ech nerve cosimplicial object]
452	  \label{def:cech_nerve_cosimplicial}
453	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechNerveCosimplicial}
454	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:push_pull_functor}
455	  The (non-augmented) cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module obtained by transporting the
456	  geometric {\v C}ech backbone in \((X/\mathbf{Sch})\) of
457	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} through the push--pull functor
458	  \(G\) of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_functor}: degreewise, the \(p\)-th term is the
459	  product over \((i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}\) of the \((p+1)\)-fold-intersection
460	  pushforwards \((j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\). This is the
461	  underlying cosimplicial object that the {\v C}ech nerve of
462	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} augments by the structure map \(\mathcal{F} \to
463	  \mathcal{C}^0\).
464	\end{definition}
465	
466	\begin{definition}\leanok
467	[Relative {\v C}ech complex from a nerve]
468	  \label{def:relative_cech_complex_of_nerve}
469	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.relativeCechComplexOfNerve}
470	  \uses{def:cech_nerve}
471	  Given \(f : X \to S\) and an augmented cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
472	  \(N\), the \emph{relative {\v C}ech cochain complex} in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\)
473	  obtained by forgetting the augmentation of \(N\), pushing the resulting
474	  cosimplicial object forward degreewise along \(f\), and taking the
475	  alternating-coface-map cochain complex. This passage uses only the preadditivity
476	  of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) and \emph{no} pushforward/pullback coherence; it is
477	  the coherence-free plumbing (3) that turns the nerve of
478	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} into the {\v C}ech complex.
479	\end{definition}

481	\section{Affine acyclicity of the {\v C}ech complex}
482	
483	\begin{definition}
484	[Standard affine cover from a spanning family]
485	  \label{def:standard_affine_cover}
486	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}
487	  \mathlibok
488	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open}).}
489	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring (so \(R = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R,
490	  \mathcal{O})\)) and let \(s : \iota \to R\) be a family of elements that generate
491	  the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\). The
492	  \emph{standard affine open cover} of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) associated with
493	  \(s\) is the affine open cover whose index set is \(\iota\), whose \(i\)-th member
494	  is the basic open \(D(s_i)\), and whose \(i\)-th structure morphism is the map of
495	  affine schemes induced by the localisation homomorphism \(R \to R_{s_i}\); that
496	  is, in Mathlib notation the defining equation
497	  \(\bigl(\text{affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}\bigr)_f\, i =
498	  \operatorname{Spec.map}\bigl(\text{algebraMap}\; R\; (\operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i))\bigr)\),
499	  so that the sections over the \(i\)-th piece are the \emph{away} localisation
500	  \(R_{s_i} = \operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i)\) and the geometric piece is
501	  \(D(s_i)\). This is exactly the standard open covering
502	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R = \bigcup_i D(s_i)\) by basic opens, packaged as a single
503	  cover object. It is the spanning-family bundle \((s, hs)\) that the affine
504	  {\v C}ech vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is stated over.
505	\end{definition}
506	
507	\begin{lemma}
508	
509	\leanok
510	[Standard-cover {\v C}ech-complex vanishing on affines]
511	  \label{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
512	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechAcyclic.affine,
513	    AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.combDifferential,
514	    AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy,
515	    AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy_zero,
516	    AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_succAbove_succ,
517	    AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy_spec,
518	    AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle,
519	    AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.combSign_flip,
520	    AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.combDifferential_comp,
521	    AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.combDifferential_exact}
522	  \uses{def:cech_complex, def:standard_affine_cover}
523	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
524	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover
525	  % {\v C}ech vanishing) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L44--52).
526	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
527	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
528	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$ be a standard
529	  % open covering of an affine open of $X$.
530	  % Then $\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for
531	  % all $p > 0$."
532	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
533	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover {\v C}ech
534	  vanishing).}
535	  Let \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) be an affine scheme, \(\mathcal{F}\) a
536	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module, and
537	  \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) a standard open cover (so the
538	  \(f_i \in A\) generate the unit ideal). The cover is presented as the
539	  spanning-family bundle \((s : \iota \to A,\; hs : \operatorname{Ideal.span}
540	  (\operatorname{range} s) = \top)\) of Definition~\ref{def:standard_affine_cover}
541	  rather than as a general open cover, so the index set and the affine pieces
542	  \(D(s_i)\) with sections \(A_{s_i}\) are read off directly from the spanning
543	  family. Then the {\v C}ech complex
544	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
545	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} has vanishing cohomology in all positive
546	  degrees:
547	  \[
548	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad \text{for all } p > 0.
549	  \]
550	  Equivalently, the extended complex
551	  \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}}
552	  \to \cdots\) of localisations is exact. This is the standard-cover {\v C}ech
553	  vanishing only; the sheaf-cohomology Serre vanishing
554	  \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) is the separate
555	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} below, obtained from this complex
556	  vanishing by the basis-comparison Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}.
557	\end{lemma}

560	% In other words, $f_1, \ldots, f_n$ are elements of $A$
561	% which generate the unit ideal of $A$.

563	% ...
564	% It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})

566	% Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}.
567	% In fact we will show that the extended complex localized
568	% at $\mathfrak p$ is homotopic to zero.
569	% There exists an index $i$ such that $f_i \not \in \mathfrak p$.
570	% Choose and fix such an element $i_{\text{fix}}$. ...

572	% $$
573	% h :
574	% \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}}
575	% M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_{p + 1}}, \mathfrak p}
576	% \longrightarrow
577	% \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p}
578	% M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_p}, \mathfrak p}
579	% $$
580	% by the rule
581	% $$
582	% h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}
583	% $$
584	% ... We compute ... $(dh + hd)(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ ...
585	% This proves the identity map is homotopic to zero as desired."
586	\begin{proof}
587	  \uses{def:cech_complex, def:standard_affine_cover}
588	  Write \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) for an
589	  \(A\)-module \(M\).
590	
591	  \paragraph{Identification of the abstract {\v C}ech complex with the localisation
592	  complex (the categorical--module bridge).}
593	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Lemma \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
594	  % items (4)--(5) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L691--719).
595	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "(4) For every $f\in R$ we have $\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde M) = M_f$
596	  % as an $R_f$-module.
597	  % (5) Whenever $D(g) \subset D(f)$ the restriction mappings
598	  % on $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$ and $\widetilde M$
599	  % are the maps $R_f \to R_g$ and $M_f \to M_g$ from Lemma
600	  % \ref{lemma-standard-open}."
601	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
602	  items (4)--(5).}
603	  The {\v C}ech complex of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} is built from the
604	  \emph{abstract} cover \(\mathcal{U} = (\text{affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}\;
605	  s\; hs).\mathrm{openCover}\) of Definition~\ref{def:standard_affine_cover}, whose
606	  degree-\(p\) term is an object of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} A)\); we
607	  record explicitly how those terms and their differential become the concrete
608	  complex of localised modules that the homotopy argument operates on (writing
609	  \(s_i = f_i\) for the spanning family). First, the \(i\)-th piece of
610	  \(\mathcal{U}\) is \(\operatorname{Spec}(A_{s_i}) \to \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the
611	  open immersion of the basic open \(D(s_i)\); the \((p+1)\)-fold intersection
612	  indexed by a tuple \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) is the basic open
613	  \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
614	  \[
615	    s_\sigma \;=\; \prod_{k=0}^{p} s_{\sigma(k)}
616	      \;=\; s_{\sigma(0)} \cdots s_{\sigma(p)},
617	  \]
618	  because the basic opens of an affine satisfy \(D(s_{i_0}) \cap \cdots \cap
619	  D(s_{i_p}) = D(s_{i_0} \cdots s_{i_p})\); its sections form the \emph{away}
620	  localisation \(A_{s_\sigma} = A_{s_{\sigma(0)} \cdots s_{\sigma(p)}}\). Second,
621	  because \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) is quasi-coherent, item~(4) of the cited
622	  lemma identifies its sections over each basic open with the away localisation of
623	  the module,
624	  \[
625	    \Gamma\bigl(D(s_\sigma), \mathcal{F}\bigr) \;=\; M_{s_\sigma},
626	  \]
627	  the localisation of \(M\) at the multiplicative set generated by the product
628	  \(s_\sigma\) (the \(\operatorname{Localization.Away}\) /
629	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) localisation of \(M\) at \(s_\sigma\)). Hence
630	  the degree-\(p\) term of the abstract {\v C}ech complex is, term by term, the
631	  product of away localisations
632	  \[
633	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
634	      \;=\; \prod_{\sigma : \{0,\ldots,p\} \to \iota}
635	              \Gamma\bigl(D(s_\sigma), \mathcal{F}\bigr)
636	      \;=\; \prod_{\sigma : \{0,\ldots,p\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma}.
637	  \]
638	  Third, the {\v C}ech differential of the abstract complex --- the alternating sum
639	  \(\sum_{j=0}^{p+1}(-1)^j\) of the restriction maps along the face inclusions
640	  \(D(s_\sigma) \hookrightarrow D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})\) obtained by omitting the
641	  \(j\)-th index (so \(D(s_\sigma) \subset D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})\), the product
642	  \(s_\sigma\) carrying the extra factor \(s_{\sigma(j)}\)) --- is, by item~(5) of
643	  the cited lemma (the restriction of \(\widetilde M\) along an inclusion of basic
644	  opens \(D(g) \subset D(f)\) is precisely the localisation map \(M_f \to M_g\)),
645	  exactly the alternating sum of the localisation maps
646	  \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\) between the away localisations. The
647	  degreewise identification of the second step therefore intertwines the abstract
648	  {\v C}ech coboundary with the concrete localised coboundary: it is an isomorphism
649	  of cochain complexes from \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
650	  onto the concrete complex of away localisations. Consequently the positive-degree
651	  vanishing \(\operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)\)
652	  of the abstract complex is equivalent to positive-degree exactness of the concrete
653	  localised complex, and it is the latter that the local-to-global criterion and the
654	  contracting homotopy below establish.
655	
656	  By Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} the {\v C}ech complex of
657	  the standard cover is therefore the complex of localisations
658	  \[
659	    \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \;\to\;
660	    \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \;\to\;
661	    \prod_{i_0 i_1 i_2} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1} f_{i_2}} \;\to\; \cdots,
662	  \]
663	  and the claim \(\check{H}^p = 0\) for \(p > 0\) is equivalent to exactness of the
664	  extended complex \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \cdots\) (whose
665	  truncation is studied in Algebra, Lemma \texttt{algebra-lemma-cover-module}).
666	  Exactness may be checked after localising at an arbitrary prime
667	  \(\mathfrak{p} \subset A\) (Algebra, Lemma
668	  \texttt{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}). Choose an index
669	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) with \(f_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \notin \mathfrak{p}\); then
670	  \(M_{f_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}, \mathfrak{p}} = M_{\mathfrak{p}}\), and the
671	  prescription \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\)
672	  defines a contracting homotopy of the localised extended complex: a direct
673	  computation gives \((dh + hd)(s) = s\), so the identity is homotopic to zero and
674	  the localised complex is exact. As exactness holds after every localisation, the
675	  extended complex is exact and \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for
676	  \(p > 0\).
677	
678	  Concretely, exactness is established node-by-node from the spanning family
679	  \((s, hs)\) of Definition~\ref{def:standard_affine_cover}: the local-to-global
680	  exactness criterion for a span of the unit ideal localises the complex at the
681	  spanning elements (the away localisations \(A_{s_r}\)), reducing positive-degree
682	  exactness of the extended complex to exactness of each localised complex; in the
683	  localisation \(A_{s_r}\) the fixed index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} = r\) makes
684	  \(s_r\) invertible, so the contracting homotopy
685	  \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{r\, i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is defined globally on that
686	  localisation and contracts it.
687	\end{proof}
688	
689	\subsection{Presheaf-level {\v C}ech machinery}
690	\label{sec:presheaf_cech_machinery}
691	
692	The {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
693	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} below) is not a statement about a single
694	affine cover: it is proved on the abelian category \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
695	of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, where the {\v C}ech complex of sections
696	is the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex of an explicit free resolution. The argument
697	rests on two presheaf-level facts, recorded here, and one transfer of injectivity.
698	First, a free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of presheaves of modules whose
699	\(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex into \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{is} the {\v C}ech complex
700	of sections (Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex},
701	Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and
702	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification}). Second, the resolution property of
703	that complex: \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) resolves \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\)
704	concentrated in degree \(0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso}). Together
705	these give: an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is injective as a presheaf
706	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} via
707	Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} applied to the sheafification adjunction
708	Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), so \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) is
709	exact and carries the augmented resolution to an exact section complex, i.e.\
710	\(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\). This direct route uses
711	neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
712	\(\delta\)-functor identification.
713	
714	\begin{definition}
715	[Free presheaf complex of a cover]
716	  \label{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
717	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex}
718	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into}
719	  % (the complex $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$) (read from
720	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1142--1162, with the $j_{p!}$ description
721	  % L1095--1135).
722	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Consider the chain complex $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$
723	  % of presheaves of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules
724	  % $$
725	  % \ldots
726	  % \to
727	  % \bigoplus_{i_0i_1i_2} (j_{i_0i_1i_2})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0i_1i_2}}
728	  % \to
729	  % \bigoplus_{i_0i_1} (j_{i_0i_1})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0i_1}}
730	  % \to
731	  % \bigoplus_{i_0} (j_{i_0})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0}}
732	  % \to 0 \to \ldots
733	  % $$
734	  % where the last nonzero term is placed in degree $0$
735	  % and where the map
736	  % $$
737	  % (j_{i_0\ldots i_{p + 1}})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0\ldots i_{p + 1}}}
738	  % \longrightarrow
739	  % (j_{i_0\ldots \hat i_j \ldots i_{p + 1}})_{p!}
740	  % \mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0\ldots \hat i_j \ldots i_{p + 1}}}
741	  % $$
742	  % is given by $(-1)^j$ times the canonical map."
743	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
744	  \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into} (the free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\)).}
745	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an
746	  open covering, with \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\)
747	  the open immersion of each finite intersection. The Stacks formulation uses, for
748	  each intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\), the \emph{extension-by-zero} presheaf
749	  \((j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{!}\,\mathcal{O}_X|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\), the presheaf of
750	  modules sending an open \(W\) to \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when
751	  \(W \subset U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) and to \(0\) otherwise. This extension-by-zero
752	  presheaf is, canonically, the \emph{free presheaf of modules on the representable}
753	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\): writing
754	  \(\mathbf{y}\,V = \operatorname{Hom}(-, V)\) for the Yoneda presheaf of the open
755	  \(V\) on the site of opens of \(X\), the free presheaf of modules
756	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V)\) sends \(W\) to the free
757	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\)-module on the set \(\operatorname{Hom}(W, V)\), which is
758	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) if \(W \subset V\) and \(0\) otherwise — exactly
759	  extension-by-zero of \(\mathcal{O}_X|_V\). Its defining universal property
760	  \[
761	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}\bigl(
762	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V),\, \mathcal{F}\bigr)
763	    \;\cong\; \mathcal{F}(V)
764	  \]
765	  is the free–forgetful adjunction for presheaves of modules composed with the
766	  Yoneda isomorphism \(\operatorname{Hom}(\mathbf{y}\,V, G) \cong G(V)\). The
767	  \emph{free presheaf complex} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the chain complex
768	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) in the abelian category
769	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose
770	  term in degree \(p\) is the direct sum
771	  \[
772	    K(\mathcal{U})_p
773	      = \bigoplus_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
774	        \operatorname{free}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
775	  \]

63	  `Functor.IsRightDerivedFunctor` (`...Functor.Derived.RightDerived`).
64	
65	**Confirmed ABSENT (all `#synth`-failed in the project's universes):**
66	- `IsGrothendieckAbelian (PresheafOfModules R)` — and `IsGrothendieckAbelian (C ⥤ D)`
67	  for `D` Grothendieck is *also* absent (no functor-category transfer).
68	- `AB5 (PresheafOfModules R)` (exactness of filtered colimits) — absent.
69	- `EnoughInjectives (PresheafOfModules R)` / `HasInjectiveResolutions (PresheafOfModules R)` — absent.
70	- Present, by contrast: `HasColimits` and `HasFilteredColimits` for `PresheafOfModules R`.
71	- No `j_!` extension-by-zero for presheaves/sheaves of modules; no Čech complex of
72	  presheaves of modules; no Čech-exactness/contractibility lemma.
73	
74	## Decisions identified
75	
76	### Decision 1: `cechFreePresheafComplex` — category + the `j_!` shriek
77	
78	- **Mathlib idiom**: the category is **`X.PresheafOfModules`** (no parallel needed).
79	  For the shriek: Mathlib has **no** `j_!` extension-by-zero for presheaves of
80	  modules, BUT it has the canonical representing-object machine
81	  **`PresheafOfModules.free`**. The blueprint summand
82	  `(j_{i_0…i_p})_! (O_X|_{U_{i_0…i_p}})` is, up to canonical iso, exactly
83	  **`(PresheafOfModules.free R).obj (yoneda.obj U_{i_0…i_p})`**: on `V` it is the
84	  free `R(V)`-module on `Hom(V,U)` = `R(V)` if `V ⊆ U`, else `0` — precisely
85	  extension-by-zero of `O_X|_U`. Its defining property
86	  `Hom(free(yoneda U), F) ≅ F(U)` is `freeAdjunction` + Yoneda. `yoneda` on
87	  `Opens ↥X` is verified to typecheck (`Opens ↥X ⥤ (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u`).
88	- **Project's proposed path**: a definition named `cechFreePresheafComplex` building
89	  `⨁ (j_…)_! O|_{U_…}` "with the alternating Čech differential". The blueprint prose
90	  introduces a bespoke `j_!` ("the shriek functor … `(j_!G)(W)=G(W)` if `W⊆U` else 0").
91	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost **iff** a bespoke `j_!`/extension-by-zero functor is
92	  authored. ALIGNED iff `j_!(O|_U)` is realized as `free(yoneda U)`.
93	- **Cost of divergence**: a hand-rolled `j_!` is a parallel API to `free`. It would
94	  need its own adjunction-with-restriction lemma to recover
95	  `Hom(j_! O|_U, F) = F(U)` (decision 2) — duplicating `freeAdjunction` — and its own
96	  functoriality for the index-dropping maps. Using `free` gives both for free: the
97	  differentials are `free.map` of the representable index-dropping maps
98	  `yoneda(U_{i_0…i_{p+1}}) → yoneda(U_{i_0…î_j…})` (inclusion of opens), and the
99	  Yoneda identity is one adjunction iso.
100	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB on the building blocks (`X.PresheafOfModules`,
101	  `free`, `yoneda`, `evaluation`); NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL for the assembled complex
102	  object (no Čech-of-presheaf-modules complex exists). Build it as a
103	  `ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ` (degree `p` = `⨁_{I^{p+1}} free(yoneda U_…)`,
104	  homological/chain variance per the blueprint's `K(𝒰)_•`). Do NOT introduce a
105	  named `j_!`.
106	
107	### Decision 2: `cechComplex_hom_identification` — `Hom(K_•, F) = Č•(𝒰,F)`
108	
109	- **Mathlib idiom**: `(freeAdjunction R).homEquiv (yoneda.obj U) F` composed with the
110	  Yoneda section iso (`yonedaEquiv` / representable sections) gives
111	  `Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda U), F) ≅ F(U)` naturally in `F`. The "shriek–restriction
112	  adjunction termwise" the directive asks for **is** `freeAdjunction` (the left
113	  adjoint of "underlying presheaf of types"), not a separate `j_! ⊣ restriction`.
114	  Take the product/`⨁`-`Hom` over multi-indices and match differentials.
115	- **Project's proposed path**: `cechComplex_hom_identification : Hom(K_•,F) = Č•(𝒰,F)`.
116	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost on one point — **which `Č•`**. The project already has
117	  `CechComplex` (`:737`) = the **relative** complex `∏ (f|_{U_…})_* (F|_{U_…})` in
118	  `S.Modules` (pushforward along `f`). The blueprint's `Č•(𝒰,F)` here is the
119	  **sections** complex `∏ F(U_{i_0…i_p})` of `O_X(U)`-modules — a *different, simpler*
120	  object. `Hom(K_•, F)` lands in `O_X(U)`-modules, not `S.Modules`.
121	- **Cost of divergence**: if `cechComplex_hom_identification` is stated against the
122	  relative `CechComplex`, the types won't line up and a bridge between "section Čech
123	  complex" and "relative pushforward Čech complex" gets invented — a parallel `Č•`.
124	  Recommendation: introduce the **presheaf section Čech complex** `Č•(𝒰,F)` as its own
125	  `CochainComplex (ModuleCat (O_X U)) ℕ` (or as `(evaluation _ (op U)).mapHomologicalComplex`
126	  applied to nothing — it is literally the `Hom`-complex), and reconcile with the
127	  relative `CechComplex` only where actually needed downstream.
128	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB (use `freeAdjunction` + Yoneda; reuse `evaluation`),
129	  with a FLAG to keep the section-`Č•` and the relative-`CechComplex` distinct rather
130	  than forking a third notion.
131	
132	### Decision 3: `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` — `K_•` resolves `O_𝒰[0]`
133	
134	- **Mathlib idiom**: none specific. Exactness of a complex of presheaves is checked
135	  objectwise (`PresheafOfModules` (co)limits and homology are objectwise); the
136	  sectionwise extended complex over each `W` is contractible by the explicit homotopy
137	  `h(s)_{i_0…} = (i_0=i_fix) s_{i_1…}`. Mathlib has `Homotopy`/`HomotopyEquiv` and
138	  `Preadditive`/homology API but not this Čech contractibility. (`ExtraDegeneracy`
139	  exists but is simplicial/chain and has the same global-`i_fix` mismatch noted in
140	  `p3-localisation.md`; do not route through it.)
141	- **Gap**: divergent-and-necessary (no idiom).
142	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL. Build the sectionwise contracting homotopy.
143	
144	### Decision 4: `presheafModules_enoughInjectives`
145	
146	- **Mathlib idiom**: the intended `IsGrothendieckAbelian.enoughInjectives` engine is
147	  real, BUT the prerequisite `IsGrothendieckAbelian (PresheafOfModules R)` is **absent**,
148	  and there is **no functor-category transfer** (`IsGrothendieckAbelian (C ⥤ D)` for
149	  `D` Grothendieck is also absent), and **`AB5 (PresheafOfModules R)` is absent**. Only
150	  `HasColimits`/`HasFilteredColimits` are present. So the cheap "instance already
151	  there" hope is false.
152	- **Project's proposed path**: derive enough-injectives from Grothendieck-abelian.
153	- **Gap**: divergent-and-necessary, **and expensive**.
154	- **Cost**: the cheapest real path is `IsGrothendieckAbelian.mk` supplying (i) `AB5`
155	  (objectwise from `ModuleCat`'s AB5 — needs an objectwise-filtered-colimit-exactness
156	  argument), (ii) a separating family (the `free(yoneda U)` objects separate), plus
157	  LocallySmall. That is a multi-lemma build. **KEY STRATEGIC NOTE:** this lemma is
158	  **NOT on the critical path to `injective_cech_acyclic`** (decision 6) — see below —
159	  so the project should weigh building it at all.
160	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (high cost; reconsider necessity).
161	
162	### Decision 5: `cech_delta_functor_presheaves`
163	
164	- **Mathlib idiom**: `Functor.rightDerived` exists but **requires
165	  `HasInjectiveResolutions (PresheafOfModules R)`**, i.e. it *depends on decision 4*.
166	  The universal-δ-functor/effaceability identification (`Ȟ^p ≅ R^p Ȟ^0`) is NOT a
167	  packaged Mathlib theorem in usable form: `IsRightDerivedFunctor` is
168	  localization-flavored and matching Čech cohomology to it is substantial. Mathlib's
169	  cohomological-δ-functor universality API is thin.
170	- **Gap**: divergent-and-necessary, expensive, and **chained behind decision 4**.
171	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (high cost; also not needed for decision 6).
172	
173	### Decision 6: `injective_cech_acyclic`
174	
175	- **Mathlib idiom (two independent parts):**
176	  1. *injective sheaf ⟹ injective presheaf*: ALIGNED — apply
177	     **`Injective.injective_of_adjoint`** to `sheafificationAdjunction` (left adjoint
178	     `sheafification`, right adjoint = the inclusion `toPresheafOfModules`), with
179	     `sheafification.PreservesMonomorphisms` (it is exact). Exactly the Stacks
180	     "right adjoint of an exact left adjoint preserves injectives".
181	  2. *the vanishing itself*: it follows **directly** from decisions 2+3 + injectivity,
182	     WITHOUT decisions 4 or 5. `K_• → O_𝒰` is an exact augmented complex (decision 3);
183	     for injective `I`, `Hom(-,I)` is exact, so `Hom(K_•,I) = Č•(𝒰,I)` (decision 2) is
184	     exact in positive degrees. This is the Stacks proof of `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`
185	     and it does **not** invoke the δ-functor identification.
186	- **Gap**: ALIGNED on part 1; part 2 is light gap-fill once 2+3 exist.
187	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB on the injective-transfer; the rest is gap-fill that
188	  bypasses decisions 4–5.
189	
190	### Decision 7: P3 re-sign of `CechAcyclic.affine`
191	
192	- **Mathlib idiom**: locked in `analogies/p3-localisation.md`. Re-sign to carry
193	  `(s : ι → Γ(X,O_X)) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)` and obtain the cover via
194	  `(Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs).openCover : X.OpenCover`, fed into the
195	  (general-`OpenCover`) `CechComplex`. The same `(s,hs)` bundle drives
196	  `exact_of_isLocalized_span`. The protected `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` and
197	  `CechComplex` keep their general signatures.
198	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (confirmed; idiomatic feed is `.openCover`).
199	
200	## Recommendation
201	
202	Build the P3b block on Mathlib's **existing** presheaf-of-modules API, not a parallel
203	one. (1) Category = `X.PresheafOfModules`. (2) The blueprint's `j_!(O_X|_U)` must be
204	`(PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj U)` — do **not** author a bespoke
205	extension-by-zero functor; that is the single highest parallel-API risk, because it
206	would duplicate `freeAdjunction` (the very thing decision 2 needs) and `free`'s
207	functoriality. (3) `cechComplex_hom_identification` = `freeAdjunction.homEquiv` +
208	Yoneda + `evaluation`; state it against a *new presheaf section* `Č•(𝒰,F)` of
209	`O_X(U)`-modules, kept distinct from the existing relative `CechComplex`. (4)
210	`injective_cech_acyclic` should be routed through `Injective.injective_of_adjoint`
211	(`sheafificationAdjunction`) + the direct "exact `Hom(-,I)` kills positive Čech
212	cohomology" argument, which needs only the free-complex resolution (decisions 2–3) and
213	**bypasses `presheafModules_enoughInjectives` (4) and `cech_delta_functor_presheaves`
214	(5)**. Decisions 4 and 5 are genuine Mathlib gaps with real cost (no Grothendieck
215	instance for presheaves of modules, no functor-category transfer, no AB5, and
216	`rightDerived` is gated on enough-injectives); since the headline result (6) does not
217	need them, treat 4–5 as optional and deprioritize them. Decision 7 is the already-locked
218	P3 re-sign. Risk order: **1 (bespoke `j_!`) > 2 (forked `Č•`) > 4,5 (expensive,
219	possibly-unnecessary gaps) > 3,6 (aligned gap-fill) > 7 (confirmed)**.

I'll start by orienting myself with the project context.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12

import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits AlgebraicTopology Opposite

namespace AlgebraicGeometry
universe u
variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

#check fun (F : X.PresheafOfModules) => F.presheaf
-- determine the Ab universe
example (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : Type u))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u} := F.presheaf

noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : Type u))
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
  map_id n := by
    apply Pi.hom_ext
    intro σ
    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp]
    sorry
  map_comp f g := by
    apply Pi.hom_ext
    intro σ
    sorry

end AlgebraicGeometry

import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits AlgebraicTopology Opposite

namespace AlgebraicGeometry
universe u
variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : Type u))
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
  map_id n := by
    apply Pi.hom_ext
    intro σ
    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _), F.presheaf.map_id, Category.comp_id]
  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
    apply Pi.hom_ext

import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits AlgebraicTopology Opposite

namespace AlgebraicGeometry
universe u
variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : Type u))
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
  map_id n := by
    apply Pi.hom_ext
    intro σ
    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
    simp
  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
    apply Pi.hom_ext
    intro σ
    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom,
      OrderHom.comp_coe]
    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
    congr 1

noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : Type u))
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
  (alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)

#print axioms sectionCechComplex

end AlgebraicGeometry

example (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : Type u)) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
    (f g : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) :

end AlgebraicGeometry

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PresheafCech
open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits Opposite
namespace AlgebraicGeometry
universe u
variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

private lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply' (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : Type u))
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
    (ψ : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) :
    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F ψ
      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app (op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V)) := by
  simp only [freeYonedaHomEquiv]; rfl

example (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : Type u)) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
    (f g : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) :
    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F (f + g) = freeYonedaHomEquiv V F f + freeYonedaHomEquiv V F g := by
  rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply', freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply', freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply',
    PresheafOfModules.add_app]
  rw [show (f.app (op V) + g.app (op V)) = (f.app (op V)) + (g.app (op V)) from rfl]
  simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_add, AddMonoidHom.add_apply]
  rfl

end AlgebraicGeometry

input.new_string:
noncomputable def freeYonedaHomEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃
      (F.presheaf ⋙ forget Ab).obj (Opposite.op V) :=
  PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv.trans yonedaEquiv

/-- **Generator formula for `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The bijection
`Hom(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is evaluation of the morphism's component at `V` on the
canonical free generator `freeMk (𝟙 V)`. Recorded project-locally because this concrete
description is what makes the bijection visibly additive (`freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`) and is
unavailable as a packaged Mathlib lemma. -/
lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
    (ψ : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) :
    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F ψ
      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V)) := by
  simp only [freeYonedaHomEquiv]; rfl

/-- **Additive upgrade of `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The free–Yoneda hom bijection
`Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is an isomorphism of abelian groups: the source is
the (preadditive) hom-group of presheaves of modules and the target is the section group
`F(V)`. This is the per-multi-index additive core of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
(blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`); taking products over multi-indices and
intertwining with the differential upgrades it to the full complex isomorphism. Additivity
holds because, by `freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply`, the bijection is evaluation of the
component `ψ.app (op V)` (a module map, hence additive) on a fixed generator, and the
component of a sum of morphisms is the sum of components.

Project-local because Mathlib packages the underlying bijection (`freeHomEquiv` ∘
`yonedaEquiv`) only at the level of types, not as an `AddEquiv`. -/
noncomputable def freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃+
      (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) where
  toFun := freeYonedaHomEquiv V F
  invFun := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).symm
  left_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).left_inv
  right_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).right_inv
  map_add' f g := by
    rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply,
      PresheafOfModules.add_app]
    change (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (f.app (Opposite.op V) + g.app (Opposite.op V))) _ = _
    rw [ModuleCat.hom_add]; rfl

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section {\v C}ech complex -/

/-- **Cosimplicial abelian group underlying the section {\v C}ech complex.**

For a family of opens `U : ι → Opens X` (an open covering) and a presheaf of modules `F`,
the cosimplicial object in `Ab` whose value in cosimplicial degree `p` is the product
`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of sections over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections. A
cosimplicial map `f : [m] ⟶ [n]` acts at multi-index `σ : Fin (n+1) → ι` by projecting to
the coordinate `σ ∘ f` and then restricting along `⨅ₖ U (σ k) ≤ ⨅ₗ U (σ (f l))`. This is the
covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
complex.

Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
as an honest `SimplexCategory ⥤ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
`d² = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
  map_id n := by
    apply Pi.hom_ext
    intro σ
    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
    simp
  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
    apply Pi.hom_ext
    intro σ
    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
    congr 1

/-- **Section {\v C}ech cochain complex of a presheaf of modules**
(blueprint `def:section_cech_complex`).

The cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F)` of abelian groups with degree-`p` term
`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` and the alternating-sum-of-restrictions differential,
obtained as the alternating-coface-map complex of `sectionCechCosimplicial`. This is the
section complex of the blueprint, kept distinct from the relative pushforward `CechComplex`;
it is the right-hand side of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.

Project-local: no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules exists in Mathlib. -/
noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
  (alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)

end AlgebraicGeometry

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/PresheafCech.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added: 4** (all axiom-clean, kernel-only `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`):
  - `sectionCechCosimplicial` (def, line 302) — helper
  - `sectionCechComplex` (def, line 333) — **primary objective `def:section_cech_complex`**
  - `freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply` (lemma, line 255) — helper (generator formula)
  - `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv` (def, line 273) — additive upgrade of `freeYonedaHomEquiv`
- **Declarations blocked: 1** — `cechComplex_hom_identification`: blocked on the cross-file
  declaration `cechFreePresheafComplex` (lives in `FreePresheafComplex.lean`, owned by another
  prover; not authorable/referenceable from here).
- **sorry count: 0 → 0** across the file (was 0 with 2 decls; now 0 with 6 decls).

## sectionCechComplex (line 333) + sectionCechCosimplicial (line 302)
- **Approach:** Built the section Čech complex as the planner directed: as a
  `CosimplicialObject Ab.{u}` (`sectionCechCosimplicial`), then applied
  `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}` to get `d²=0` for free.
  - Input chosen: a family of opens `{ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens X)` (NOT `X.OpenCover`) and
    `F : X.PresheafOfModules`. This matches the blueprint `def:section_cech_complex`
    (`∏ F(U_{i₀…iₚ})`), the existing in-file `freeYonedaHomEquiv` (which takes `V : Opens X`),
    and the intended `cechFreePresheafComplex` (free side over the same opens).
  - Codomain category: `Ab.{u}` (= `AddCommGrp.{u}`). Verified `F.presheaf : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}`,
    so sections `F.presheaf.obj (op V)` are naturally abelian groups; the Hom-complex
    `Hom_{PMod}(K_•, F)` is also naturally in `Ab`, so this is the correct target (and is more
    honest than "O_X(U)-modules" for the hom-identification — flag for blueprint reconcile).
  - Degree-`p` object: `∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len+1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))`.
    `Ab` has all products; `Pi.lift`/`Pi.π`/`Pi.lift_π`/`Pi.hom_ext` used.
  - Cosimplicial map at `σ`: project to coordinate `σ ∘ f.toOrderHom`, then restrict via
    `F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l))).op`.
  - `map_id`: `Pi.hom_ext`, `simp [Pi.lift_π, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom, OrderHom.id_coe,
    Function.comp_id]`, then `Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)` (homs in `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ` are
    a subsingleton) + `simp`.
  - `map_comp`: `Pi.hom_ext`, `simp [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom,
    OrderHom.comp_coe]`, reassociate + `Pi.lift_π`, `← Functor.map_comp`, `congr 1` (the residual
    restriction-hom equality closes by proof-irrelevance / subsingleton homs).
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean (`lean_verify` on `sectionCechComplex` → kernel axioms only).

## freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply (line 255) + freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (line 273)
- **Approach (requested "upgrade `freeYonedaHomEquiv` to additive"):**
  - First proved the generator formula `freeYonedaHomEquiv V F ψ =
    (ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app (op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V))` (`simp only
    [freeYonedaHomEquiv]; rfl`). Derived from the Mathlib defs `freeHomEquiv ψ =
    freeAdjunctionUnit ≫ whiskerRight (toPresheaf.map ψ)` and `yonedaEquiv_apply`
    (eval at `𝟙 V`), with `freeAdjunctionUnit.app _ x = ModuleCat.freeMk x`.
  - `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv : (free(yoneda V) ⟶ F) ≃+ F.presheaf.obj (op V)`: underlying bijection
    is `freeYonedaHomEquiv`; `map_add'` via the generator formula + `PresheafOfModules.add_app`
    (`(f+g).app = f.app + g.app`) + bridge `change (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (… + …)) _ = _` then
    `ModuleCat.hom_add; rfl` (note: the term is `ConcreteCategory.hom`, defeq but not syntactically
    `ModuleCat.Hom.hom`, hence the `change`).
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean (`lean_verify` on `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv` → kernel axioms only).

## cechComplex_hom_identification (NOT added)
- **Approach 1 (full complex iso):** needs the LHS `Hom_{PMod}(cechFreePresheafComplex U, F)`,
  but `cechFreePresheafComplex` is in `FreePresheafComplex.lean` (another prover's file). I cannot
  author or reference it without editing a file I do not own → BLOCKED on cross-file dependency.
- **Approach 2 (file-independent degree-wise brick):** a generic
  `(⨁_σ free(yoneda ⨅σ) ⟶ F) ≃+ ∏_σ F(⨅σ)` would feed the hom-identification. Investigated:
  - Mathlib has **no** packaged coproduct/biproduct hom-equiv (`Sigma.homEquiv`,
    `biproduct.homEquiv` absent; confirmed by env scan). The analogy file's note holds.
  - `preadditiveCoyoneda` is the **wrong variance** (covariant `Hom(F,−)`); the contravariant
    `preadditiveYoneda.obj F` preserves limits and would send `op(∐A)` to a product, but

`PresheafCech.lean` (P3b section side) iter-016: 4 axiom-clean decls added.
`sectionCechComplex` (the `def:section_cech_complex` objective), `sectionCechCosimplicial`,
`freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply`, `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`. See [[p3-cechacyclic-l3-done]].

**Reusable recipe (cosimplicial Čech in Ab):**
- Input: `{ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens X)` + `F : X.PresheafOfModules` (a family of opens, NOT
  `X.OpenCover`). Target category `Ab.{u}` (`F.presheaf : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}`).
- Build `CosimplicialObject Ab.{u}` by hand: obj `n` = `∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len+1) → ι =>
  F.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))`; map `f` = `Pi.lift (fun σ => Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
  F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l))).op)`.
- `map_id`/`map_comp` via `Pi.hom_ext` + `Pi.lift_π` + `SimplexCategory.{id,comp}_toOrderHom`;
  the residual restriction-hom equalities close because homs in `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ` are a
  **Subsingleton** (`Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)`, or `congr 1` via proof-irrelevance).
- Then `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}` gives `d²=0` for free.
- Additivity of `freeYonedaHomEquiv`: generator formula `= (ψ.app (op V)).hom (ModuleCat.freeMk
  (𝟙 V))` (`simp only [freeYonedaHomEquiv]; rfl`), then `PresheafOfModules.add_app` + `change` to
  `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` + `ModuleCat.hom_add` (note `ConcreteCategory.hom` ≠ syntactically `.hom`).

**Blocker:** `cechComplex_hom_identification` needs `cechFreePresheafComplex` (free side, in
`FreePresheafComplex.lean`, different prover). Mathlib has NO coproduct/biproduct-hom equiv;
`preadditiveCoyoneda` is wrong variance (use `preadditiveYoneda` preservation for the joint step).
